12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Micah </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>2</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:7b,
12-13;</span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'> 3</span></b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:4, 5-7; <b>5</b>:6; <b>7</b>:4, 15,
18, 19 </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:.5in'><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Zephaniah <b>1</b>:17;
Zechariah <b>7</b>:13, 14; </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>8</span></b><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:22; <b>10</b>:6, 11;
<b>12</b>:10; Malachi <b>1</b>:9. Our longest persons shift occurs in Isaiah </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>59</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:1-15,
which begins with <i>you</i> (1-3), continues with <i>they</i> (4-8) and closes
with <i>we</i> (9-15).</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify;text-indent:
.5in'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>The
complexity of this issue can be confusing as illustrated by Jeremiah </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>30</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:7b-9.
<i>CV</i> has taken considerable liberties with what is evidently a variance in
textual transmission on top of discontinuity of person and number. The Greek
LXX reading varies from the Hebrew, and the Hebrew evidently exhibits a dual
discontinuity. The following treatment captures the threefold complexity (<i><s>Hebrew</s></i><s>/LXX</s></span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>/</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>CV):
<i>“He [Jacob] will be saved from it . . .</i> </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>I’ll
break his </span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>[Jacob’s]<i> yoke from off <s>your/<b>their</b></s></i><b>/</b><i>his
neck and tear off <s>your/<b>their</b>/his</s> its straps. Foreigners won’t enslave
him anymore. </i></span><b><sup><span style='font-size:14.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>9</span></sup></b><i><s><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>They’ll</span></s></i><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>/He’ll
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>[Jacob
as a collective] <i>serve Yahveh <s>their</s>/his God and David <s>you</s>r/his
king that I’ll raise up for <s>them</s>/him.” </i>The result makes a true
statement even if it fails to represent the form of the intended true statement
in the original.<i> </i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify;text-indent:
.5in'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            We
have kept the discontinuity in some poetry (Psalm <b>30</b>:3, 4, <i>etc.</i>).</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify;text-indent:
.5in'><b><span lang=DE style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>tense</span></b><span lang=DE style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>: Genesis <b>2</b>:8; Exodus
</span><b><span lang=DE style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>15</span></b><span lang=DE style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:6-7; Deuteronomy <b>33</b>:27;
Psalm <b>94</b>:23; <b>95</b>:10; <b>132</b>:6, 17; Isaiah <b>26</b>:15; <b>32</b>:14
check; </span><b><span lang=DE style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>37</span></b><span lang=DE
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:24;
Ezekiel<b> 7</b>:19</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify;text-indent:
.5in'><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>number</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:
Deuteronomy <b>9</b>:4; Psalm <b>10</b>:16; <b>22</b>:29; <b>35</b>: 8; <b>49</b>:12,
20; <b>57</b>:4; <b>92</b>:14;<b> 103</b></span><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:15-16;<b> 123</b>:2; <b>127</b>:5;
Proverbs<b> 6</b>:1-19; Isaiah <b>10</b>:25; <b>44</b>:7; <b>46</b>:7; Jeremiah
<b>49</b>:20-21; Ezekiel <b>5</b>:14; Hosea <b>7</b>:11-16; <b>10</b>:2; <b>13</b>:14;
Micah<b> 2</b>:9</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                        Discontinuity
of both number and person shows up in Nahum <b>1</b>:13.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify;text-indent:
.5in'><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>viewpoint</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:
<i>CV</i> tries to maintain viewpoint by choosing between <i>go/came</i> and <i>take/bring</i>
to fit the viewpoint in the previous context: Psalm<b> 121</b>:8.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify;text-indent:
.5in'><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>sequence</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> English
custom builds to a climax; Hebrew tends to start with the climax and come down
from it. We have reversed the sequence in a few cases where that seems to be
the rationale for the order in the text. </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                        <b>size
     </b></span><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>1 Kings<b> 7</b>:10, expensive stones 15ft and 12ft
reordered to 12ft and 15ft</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:1.0in;text-align:justify;text-indent:.5in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Judges
<b>18</b>:19 tribe-family to family-tribe;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                                    2
Chronicles<b> 28</b></span><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>: Solomon’s Prayer of Dedication</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify;text-indent:.5in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                        <i>“least
to greatest”</i> from <i>“greatest to least”</i> (Jonah </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>3</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:5)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                                    Zechariah<b>
5</b>:2?: 30 x 15 feet changed to 15 x 30ft</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                        <b>degree
</b>See also Exodus <b>15</b>:16 (<i>“panic and dread”</i> changed to <i>“dread
and panic”</i>);</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                                    <u>most
holy and holy</u> to<u> holy and most holy</u> (Leviticus </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>21</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:22).</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                                    See
also 2 Kings </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>12</span></b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:10; Isaiah </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>40</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:30.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:1.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.5in'><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>time</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
designation 2 Kings <b>25</b>:27 (year, month, day changed to American
practice: day, month, year)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                                    Job
<b>41</b>:61; Psalm <b>40</b>:5, 6; Proverbs <b>24</b>:7; <b>34</b>:27; Isaiah <b>27</b>:6;
<b>40</b>:30;<b> 52</b>:2</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:1.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.5in'><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>direction
</span></b><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“head
to toe”</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'> instead of <i>“foot to head”</i> (Isaiah </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>1</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:6);
note also <i>“between Beth-horon and Samaria”</i> instead of the reverse
because the direction of the troops’ movement was from south to north (2
Chronicles </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>25</span></b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:13).</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:81.0pt;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                        Change
of sequence <b>to avoid misreading</b> in English. </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:.5in'><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            Putting
an adjective, article, preposition, or phrase with the first of two or more
nouns inclines an English reader to associate it with the subsequent nouns as
well. To help avoid that problem, we have changed the order of the nouns so the
modifier appears ahead of the last noun. 2 Kings <b>1</b>:8 says, <i>“He was
wearing clothes made out of hair and a leather belt”</i>; the statement
becomes, <i>“he was wearing a leather belt and clothes made out of hair.” </i>Hebrew
can have the textual order because the possessives, prepositions, articles are
attached to each word.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:.5in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></p>

<p class=MsoListParagraph style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;
margin-bottom:0in;margin-left:63.0pt;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>   Change
of sequence for <b>English euphony and custom</b> </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:45.0pt;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                     English
custom is for speakers to put themselves second in a combined entry. That lies
behind reversing entries in Genesis <b>31</b>:42<sup>2</sup>; Exodus<b> 9</b>:27
(<i>“my people and I” </i>from <i>“me and my people”</i>); Joshua<b> 24</b>:15;
Judges <b>11</b>:37 (<i>“companions and I”</i>); 1 Kings <b>1</b>:21; 2
Chronicles <b>32</b>:13 (<i>“father and I”</i>); Esther<b> 7</b>:4; <b>9</b>:6-10;
Psalm <b>111</b>:7; Ezekiel <b>20</b>:12, 20. Original order retained in Judges
<b>12</b>:2.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:1.0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            <i>earth and sky</i>: Genesis
<b>1</b>:1, 18; Haggai <b>1</b>:6;<b> 2</b>:21. <i>Heaven and earth</i> is
natural enough; but since we have used <i>sky</i> in place of <i>heaven</i>, we
have reversed the order.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:1.0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Which
of Eden’s trees equals you in <u>size</u> and <u>glory</u>?</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
(Ezekiel <b>31</b>:1) from <i>Which of Eden’s trees equals you in <u>glory</u>
and <u>size</u>?</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                  <i>bigger and
stronger</i>: Deuteronomy </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>9</span></b><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:14</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                  <i>my King and God</i>
to <i>my God and King</i>: Psalm </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>84</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:3</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                  <i>come and go</i>:
1 Samuel <b>18</b>:13; <b>29</b>:6</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                  <i>up and down</i>:
Isaiah <b>50</b>:2</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:1.0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            <i>sword, famine, and
plague</i> <i>vs.</i> other sequences made uniform in 2 Samuel </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>6</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:2;
2 Kings </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>19</span></b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:15; 1 Chronicles </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>13</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:6;
Psalm </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>80</span></b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:1; Isaiah </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>37</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:16</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:1.0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            <i>morning and evening</i>
(Genesis 1 <i>seriatim</i>) rather than evening and morning. In Hebrew
reckoning, a day began in the evening. English parlance reflects the custom of
starting the twenty-four-hour period in the morning (technically at midnight). In
Psalm </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>55</span></b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:17, <i>evening. morning, and noon </i>changes
to <i>morning, noon, and night. </i>Note Exodus </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>16</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:8,
12.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                  <i>good and evil</i>
from <i>evil and good</i>: Leviticus<b> 5</b>:4</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                  <i>flesh and bones</i>
from <i>bones and flesh</i>: 2 Samuel </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>5</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:1;
</span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>19</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:13</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:1.0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            <i>head to toe </i>rather
than <i>foot to head</i>: Deuteronomy <b>28</b>:35; 2 Samuel </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>15</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:25;
Isaiah </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>1</span></b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:6</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:1.0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            <i>day and night</i> from<i>
night and</i> <i>day</i> (because a day was thought of as beginning at sunset?):
1 Kings <b>8</b>:29; Jeremiah <b>14</b>:7</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:1.0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            <i>left and right</i>
from <i>right and left</i>: Exodus </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>14</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:22;
1 Kings </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>7</span></b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:21; 2 Chronicles </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>3</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:17;
</span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>23</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:10;
Nehemiah </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>8</span></b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:4 (but not </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>12</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:31
+ 38); Psalm </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>26</span></b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:10; </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>142</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:4;
Proverbs</span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'> 3</span></b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:16; Ecclesiastes </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>10</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:2;
Isaiah </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>54</span></b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:3; Jonah </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>4</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:11</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                  <i>made and formed </i>from
<i>formed and made</i>: Isaiah <b>43</b>:7 </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                  <i>rich and poor</i>:
Ruth</span><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
<b>3</b>:10</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                  just
and good </span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>from <i>good and just </i>(Psalm</span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
25</span></b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:8)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:1.0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            <i>great and small</i>
from <i>small and great</i>: 2 Kings </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>23</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:1;
1 Chronicles </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>25</span></b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:8; </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>26</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:13;
Job </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>3</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:19;
Psalm </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>15</span></b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:13</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                  <i>shovel and fork</i>
becomes <i>fork and shovel</i>: Isaiah <b>30</b>:24;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                  <i>children and
women</i> becomes <i>women and children</i>: Esther<b> 8</b>:11. </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:1.0in;text-align:justify'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>             “My God” </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>and
<i>“my Lord”</i> can sound like interjections, like <i>“using the Lord’s name
in vain</i>,<i>”</i> like bywords. The whole vocative falls out in Genesis </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>12</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:11;
Daniel </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>9</span></b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:6, 8. In other places, <i>CV</i>
drops <i>my</i>, especially if the vocative starts the sentence (Daniel </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>10</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:16;
</span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>12</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:8).
In Genesis </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>44</span></b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:18; 1 Samuel </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>1</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:26;
Daniel </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>4</span></b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:19 the initial vocative is relocated
away from the front of the sentence.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:1.0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>NOTE: 
English custom of sequence for multiple adjectives ahead of the same noun tends
to apply also to the sequence of factors presented aside from that
circumstance. For example, size precedes quality: “big red barn” rather than
“red, big barn” (hence the use of commas in the “un-customary” order). The
switchover from Hebrew to English custom calls for an unconscious re-ordering
of factors beyond what natural, temporal, or logical sequence might call for.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></p>

<p class=MsoListParagraph style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;
margin-bottom:0in;margin-left:63.0pt;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Resequencing
to <b>natural order and English customary order</b></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                        <i>CV</i>
changes some <u>word orders</u> to fit English custom:  </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                                    cause
to effect: Deuteronomy<b> 16</b>:15 <i>produce-do</i> to <i>do-produce</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:1.25in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      <u>turn aside,
spend the night, wash feet</u> to <u>turn aside, wash feet, spend the night</u>
(Genesis </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>19</span></b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:2);</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:63.0pt;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>               <u>destroy
and subdue</u> to <u>subdue and destroy</u>: Deuteronomy <b>9</b>:3;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                  <i>“they
die for lack of water”</i> then <i>“The fish in them stink”</i>: Isaiah <b>50</b>:2;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:1.25in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      <i>“spread the
tablecloth and set the table”</i> from “set the table and spread the
tablecloth”: Isaiah <b>21</b>:5;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                  <i>“succeed
and build”</i> becomes <i>“succeed at building”</i>: 1 Chronicles <b>19</b>:3; <b>22</b>:11;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                  <i>“dry
land”</i> put after <i>“separated the water”</i>: Exodus <b>14</b>:21;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                  <u>take
root, sprout, blossom</u> from <u>take root, blossom, sprout</u>: Isaiah </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>27</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:6;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:1.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:.25in'><i><u><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“tied
the money in bags and counted the money”</span></u></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> to <i>“<u>counted
the money and tied it in bags”</u></i>: 2 Kings </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>12</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:10;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:1.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Joshua
</span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>6</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:26
reverses poetic lines.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:1.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-22.5pt'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>re-ordered
word sets: </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>“earth and sky”</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> rather than <i>“heaven
and earth</i>,<i>” “day and night”</i> <i>vs.</i> <i>“night and day</i>,<i>”
“you and I</i>,<i>” vs.</i> the text’s sequence, <i>“feet and hands”</i>
becomes <i>“hands and feet</i>,<i>” “life and death”</i> <i>vs.</i> <i>“death
and life”</i> (2 Samuel <b>15</b>:21), <i>“land and sea”</i> <i>vs.</i> <i>“sea
and land”</i> (Jonah <b>1</b>:9).</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:1.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-22.5pt'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>       See
also Deuteronomy <b>9</b>:23, 28; <b>11</b>:30; <b>33</b>:17; 2 Chronicles <b>6</b>:28;
Psalm <b>17</b>:9; <b>144</b>:3; Isaiah <b>19</b>:12; Jeremiah <b>15</b>:3; <b>14</b>:14;<b>
23</b>:1; Jonah <b>3</b>:5.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:1.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-22.5pt'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Joshua
</span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>3</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:4:
“</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Put
about 3,000ft </span></i><i><sup><span style='font-size:14.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>2,000 cubits</span></sup></i><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
between you and it. Don’t get close to it.” </span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>The sequence
becomes “<i>Don’t get close to it”</i> then “<i>Put about 3,000ft </i></span><i><sup><span
style='font-size:14.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>2,000
cubits</span></sup></i><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> between you and it.” </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(general
order to specific suggestion)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                          
  Obadiah 11: <u>carry off wealth, enter gates, cast lots for Jerusalem</u>
reversed</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:1.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-22.5pt'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Jeremiah<b>
32</b>:44: <i>“Men will [buy fields for money <sup>repeat</sup>]<sup> </sup>call
witnesses and sign and seal deeds <s>and call witnesses</s> in Benjamin” </i>instead
of<i> ‘sign and seal deeds and call witnesses.”</i> Calling witnesses would
precede the other parts of the transaction (cp. <b>32</b>:10).</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:1.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-22.5pt'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Daniel
<b>7</b>:11: <i>“its body was <s>destroyed and</s> thrown into the flaming fire
and destroyed.</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:1.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-22.5pt'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>See
also Genesis <b>1</b>:14; Leviticus <b>11</b>:40; Joshua <b>6</b>:18; 2 Samuel<b>
22</b>:9; Nehemiah <b>2</b>:6b; Psalm <b>71</b>:6; Isaiah <b>22</b>:17-19; <b>51</b>:22;
Jeremiah <b>25</b>:34; <b>27</b>:1; <b>31</b>:34; <b>33</b>:26; Ezekiel<b> 3</b>:10;
<b>7</b>:3; <b>20</b>:39; <b>23</b>:35; <b>35</b>:10; <b>36</b>:33; Micah<b> 1</b>:13;<b>
3</b>:3; Habakkuk <b>1</b>:7; some of the pairs in the “there’s a time for”
list in Ecclesiastes <b>3</b>:2-8</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                  Hebrew
tends to put the main element first instead of the natural, causal sequence.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Double
Entry</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            In
keeping with expansive expression, body-part imagery, and parallelism in both
poetry and narrative, the Hebrew text uses several kinds of double entry for
synonymous or near synonymous ideas perhaps to emphasize or arrest attention.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            </span></p>

<p class=MsoListParagraphCxSpFirst style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;
margin-bottom:0in;margin-left:67.5pt;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(a)<span
style='font:7.0pt "Times New Roman"'>&nbsp;&nbsp; </span></span><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Synonymous
double entry reduced to one</span></p>

<p class=MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;
margin-bottom:0in;margin-left:1.0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Judges <b>2</b></span><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>0:7
(advice <s>and counsel</s>)       </span></p>

<p class=MsoListParagraphCxSpLast style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;
margin-bottom:0in;margin-left:1.0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>2 Samuel </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>7</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:12
(<i>“When your days are over <s>and you lie down with your ancestors</s></i>)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify;text-indent:.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>2
Kings<b> 9</b>:15 (“<s>leave and</s> escape”)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify;text-indent:.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>1
Chronicles<b> 21</b>:17 (<i>“I <s>sinned and</s> did very wickedly”</i>)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify;text-indent:.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>2
Chronicles <b>7</b>:19<sup>2</sup> (“<s>serve and</s> worship” [though
different in scope])</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify;text-indent:.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>2
Chronicles <b>32</b>:16 (“<s>insult and</s> speak against” <b>check</b>)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:.5in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> 2
Chronicles <b>32</b>:18 (“<s>frighten and</s> terrify”)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:.5in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Hosea
</span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>1</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:10
(<i>“Israelites . . . you can’t <s>measure/weigh or</s> count”</i>)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:1.0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Psalm <b>40</b>:16 (“<s>rejoice
and</s> be happy”)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify;text-indent:.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Jeremiah
<b>15</b>:20 (“<s>saved you and</s> deliver you” <b>check</b>)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify;text-indent:.25in'><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Leviticus <b>11</b>:13
(<i>“<s>detest and</s> abhor”</i>); Numbers <b>14</b>:18 (<i>“<s>iniquity and</s>
sin”</i>); Deuteronomy <b>9</b>:5; <b>11</b>:2, 4, 6, 16; <b>12</b>:9, 10, 12; <b>15</b>:10;
<b>17</b>:1; <b>18</b>:22; <b>26</b>:13; <b>31</b>:21; Joshua<b> 9</b>:26; 1
Samuel <b>12</b>:14, 15; <b>25</b>:26; 1 Kings <b>13</b>:6; <b>20</b>:22 (<i>“observe
and see”</i>); 2 Chronicles <b>13</b>:6 (<i>“<s>rose up and</s> rebelled”</i>);
<b>29</b>:6 (<i>“<s>left and</s> turned back on”</i>); Nehemiah </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>4</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:20;
Esther <b>4</b>:14; Job<b> 3</b>:5; <b>6</b>:2; <b>14</b>:11; <b>16</b>:4; </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>18</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:19;
</span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>39</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:24;
Psalm <b>10</b>:15; <b>27</b>:2, 9; <b>32</b>:8; <b>35</b>:27; <b>40</b>:5;<b> 63</b>:5;
<b>70</b>:2; <b>90</b>:2; <b>91</b>:7; <b>119</b>:163; </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>121</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:3
(<i>“<s>slumber or</s> sleep”</i>); <b>130</b>:2; <b>144</b>:11; Proverbs <b>5</b>:19;
</span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>13</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:5;
Ecclesiastes <b>7</b>:20; Isaiah <b>1</b>:2; <b>10</b>:17; <b>20</b>:3; <b>27</b>:10;
<b>30</b>:7; 4<b>0</b>:30; <b>44</b>:8; <b>49</b>:17 (<i>“destroyers <s>and
devastators</s></i>); <b>49</b>:26; <b>53</b>:19; <b>65</b>: 17; Jeremiah <b>7</b>:20
(<i>“anger and wrath”</i>);<b> 9</b>:23; <b>15</b>:1; <b>16</b>:18 (“iniquity
and sin”); <b>18</b>:11; <b>23</b>:4; <b>27</b>:8, 22;<b> 33</b>:8; Ezekiel <b>13</b>:8
(<i>“falsehood and lied”</i>); <b>16</b>:3; </span><b><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>18</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:25;
<b>20</b>:44; <b>30</b>:23, 26; <b>33</b>:15; <b>36</b>:19; Daniel <b>6</b>:11;<b>
9</b>:16 (<i>“<s>anger and</s> wrath”</i>); Daniel </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>9</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:3,
17, 18, 23 (<i>“prayer <s>and supplication</s>”</i>); Hosea </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>1</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:3;
Joel <b>2</b>:20 (<i>“stench <s>and foul smell</s>”</i>); Amos <b>9</b>:19; Zechariah
</span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>1</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:4
(<i>“<s>spoke to and said</s>”/”told</i>); Malachi </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>2</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:1
(<i>“If you don’t <s>listen and</s> take it to heart”</i>)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify;text-indent:.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Antithetical Double Entry (positive statement
plus denial of opposite)  </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            Hebrew idiom has a
penchant for affirmation plus denial of opposite. As such, it is one expression
of a larger impulse toward parallelism. Proverbs <b>31</b>:12 illustrates the
idea in its familiar description of the ideal wife: <i>“She’ll do him good <u>and
not evil</u> all the days of her life.”</i> Joshua <b>1</b>:7 says, “<sup>(a)</sup><i>Be
careful to do everything in the Law that Moses commanded you;</i> <sup>(b)</sup><i>“don’t
turn from it to the right or left”</i> becomes </span><b><sup><span
style='font-size:14.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>7</span></sup></b><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“Make
sure you do everything in the Law that Moses commanded you.”</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> Jeremiah
<b>32</b>:33 says, <i>“They’ve turned their back on me<s>, not their face.</s>”</i>
See also 1 Kings <b>2</b>:36; 2 Chronicles <b>35</b>:21.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify;text-indent:.5in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> (a)
reduced to one </span></p>

<p class=MsoListParagraphCxSpFirst style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;
margin-bottom:0in;margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Genesis <b>18</b>:25;
<b>41</b>:23; <b>42</b>:1; <b>47</b>:19; Leviticus <b>22</b>:21; </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>26</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:11;
Deuteronomy <b>15</b>:8; <b>17</b>:11, 15; <b>20</b>:12, 15; <b>33</b>:5;
Joshua</span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'> 1</span></b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:7; <b>7</b>:19 (<i>“tell me . . .
don’t hide it”</i>); 1 Samuel <b>1</b>:11; <b>6</b>:3 (don’t send it away
empty; return a guilt offering); <b>14</b>:19, 45 (saved Jonathan and he didn’t
die); <b>20</b>:21; <b>24</b>:1; 1 Kings <b>3</b>:27; <b>5</b>:57 (x and not
abandon us”); <b>13</b>:21-22; </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>22</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:8,
18; 2 Kings <b>4</b>:27; <b>5</b>:3; <b>10</b>:23; <b>13</b>:5, 11; <b>18</b>:6,
32; Job </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>27</span></b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:6; Psalm <b>28</b>:5; </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>111</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:8;
<b>118</b>:17; Jeremiah</span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> 16</span></b><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:17; <b>18</b>:17;
<b>42</b>:10; Ezekiel </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>17</span></b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:14; <b>18</b>:21, 28; <b>24</b>:27;
Amos </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>5</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:14</span></p>

<p class=MsoListParagraphCxSpLast style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;
margin-bottom:0in;margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>              (b) hendiadys
(two words for one compound referent) expressed by some combined wording</span></p>

<p class=MsoListParagraph style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:1.0in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“bowed
and worshiped”</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> becomes <i>“bowed in worship”</i>: Exodus
<b>4</b>:31; <b>34</b>:8; 2 Chronicles <b>29</b>:29, 30;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                  <i>“deliver
them and throw them into confusion”</i> <i>becomes “delivered them by throwing
them </i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                        into
confusion”</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>: Deuteronomy <b>7</b>:23;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                  “<i>Job
didn’t sin or blame God” </i>becomes <i>“Job didn’t sin by blaming God” </i>(Job
</span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>1</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:22);</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                  <i>“your
harlotry and wickedness”</i> becomes <i>“your wicked harlotry”</i> (Jeremiah <b>3</b>:2);</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                  </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“they’ll
die for the treachery <s>and sin</s> they’re committing now”</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
(Ezekiel <b>18</b>:24); </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                  <i>“fell
on his face and did homage”</i> becomes <i>“fell on his face in homage”</i> (Daniel
<b>2</b>:46);</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                  <i>“tremble
and be afraid”</i> become <i>“tremble in fear”</i> (Daniel <b>5</b>:19);</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                  <i>“to
seal up </i>[fulfill?] vision and prophecy” becomes <i>“prophetic vision” </i>(Daniel
</span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>9</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:24);</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                  See
also Genesis </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>18</span></b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:7; <b>50</b>:18; Deuteronomy
<b>32</b>:18, 29; Job </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>1</span></b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:22.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (c) unnecessarily
detailed expression</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:1.0in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“knew
his wife and she <s>conceived and</s> bore a son”</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> becomes
<i>“had relations with his wife and she bore a son”</i>: Genesis <b>4</b>:1, <i>etc.</i>;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“<s>reached out and</s>
took hold of”</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>: 2 Samuel<b> 6</b>:6;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“humbled <s>their heart</s>/them
with labor”</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>: Psalm <b>107</b>:12.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                  <i>“You’ll
store up for safekeeping, but <s>you won’t preserve it because</s> I’ll deliver
most of it </i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                        to
the sword” </span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(Micah </span><b><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>6</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:14b)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (d)
equivalents to ingressive action</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“<s>take and</s> x”</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:
Genesis <b>29</b>:23; Deuteronomy <b>9</b>:17, 21; 1 Samuel<b> 16</b>:23; <b>25</b>:18;
2 Samuel          <b>4</b>:12; <b>18</b>:17;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“<s>arise and</s> x”</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:
Nehemiah <b>2</b>:20;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                  <i>“go
<s>and </s>x”</i>: <i>“I will go and see him before I die”</i> becomes <i>“I’ll
go see him before I die”</i>: </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                        (Genesis
<b>45</b>:28), <i>etc.</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:1.0in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.5in'><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(e) Sometimes the
double entry says the same thing two ways simply for <u>emphasis</u>: <i>“there’s
nothing devious <s>or twisted</s> in it” </i>(Proverbs <b>8</b>:8); <i>“</i>(your
words) <i>became a <s>joy and</s> delight to me”</i> (Jeremiah<b> 15</b>:16; <i>“I’ll
cleanse them from their sin against me <s>and pardon their transgression</s>” </i>(Jeremiah
<b>33</b>:8); likewise Isaiah <b>29</b>:14; Amos <b>5</b>:21, <i>“I despise
your festivals<s>; I hate them</s>;”</i> Habakkuk <b>1</b>:5, <i>et alii</i>.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (f)
Double entry can be a <u>rhetorical device</u> for balance.                                 </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      (g) In a few examples, the
double seems merely to be a <u>customary expression</u>, as in <i>“eat </i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            and drink</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>,<i>”</i>
which we have sometimes reduced simply to <i>“eat.” </i>Note also</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:1.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“marry
and given in marriage”</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> can reduce simply to <i>“get
married”</i>; the Hebrew expression probably stems from the custom of two
parents arranging a marriage for their offspring instead of the offspring
arranging their own marriage.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:1.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“see
and hear”</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'> can become <i>“see” </i>in some cases:  </span></p>

<p class=MsoListBullet style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:1.25in;text-align:justify'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“flesh and bones”</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> means
related people; in this case, <i>CV</i> lets the expression stand (Genesis <b>29</b>:14;
Judges <b>9</b>:2; 2 Samuel <b>5</b>:2; <b>19</b>:12, 13; 1 Chronicles <b>10</b>:11;
Job <b>2</b>:5) instead of shifting it to the more familiar “flesh and blood”
(cp. Matthew <b>16</b>:17).</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                        <i>“fear
and trembling”</i> (Psalm <b>55</b>:5)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                        <i>“day
and night”</i> for <i>“all the time,”</i> <i>“every day,”</i> continually,
characteristically;    </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                        <i> “word
and deed.” </i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                        <i>“from
the head to the sole of the foot”</i> to <i>“from head to toe”</i> (Isaiah <b>1</b>:6)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Idioms for Intensity</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            Cognate
constructions</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:.5in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“Slew
with slaughter”</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> becomes <i>“slaughtered”</i>: Joshua <b>10</b>:10,
20 both the same?</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:.5in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“Swear
with an oath” </span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>becomes <i>“solemnly swear”</i>: Ezekiel <b>21</b>:23.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:.5in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Burn
with fire means “burn intensely”: Exodus <b>29</b>:34</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            Double entry (See
earlier in “Hebrew-English Differences.”)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Indirect Discourse Formats</span></p>

<p class=MsoListParagraph style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:58.5pt;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(a)</span><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> 
English keeps all the parts of indirect discourse after the start of the
clause. Hebrew may put the noun ahead of the beginning and then repeat it with
a pronoun inside the clause:</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:1.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.5in'><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Genesis <b>1</b>:4<i>:
“God saw the light that it was good”</i> becomes <i>“God saw that the light was
good.”</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:1.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.5in'><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Genesis <b>6</b>:2:
<i>“The sons of god saw the daughters of men that they were beautiful”</i> becomes
<i>“The sons of god saw that the daughters of men were beautiful.”</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:63.0pt;text-align:justify;text-indent:-27.0pt'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> (b)
Direct discourse we have changed to indirect discourse in many places,
especially where it can eliminate some level of embedded quotation (one
quotation within another). The Hebrew text seems reluctant to use indirect
discourse; indirect expression sounds more natural in English and reduces the
complexity of longer passages. English speakers prefer indirect discourse over
direct quotation when talking about what someone said. The greater use of
direct quotation does have the effect of hearing the content in contrast to
hearing about it, so eliminating direct discourse entirely would not be wise. <i>CV</i>
has adjusted quite many texts into indirect statements as listed below. </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:1.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Genesis
</span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>3</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:3,
13, 14; </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>8</span></b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:16-17; </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>12</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:12,
29: </span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>14</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:23;
</span><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>16</span></b><span

12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>30</span></b><span

font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>41</span></b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:55; <b>42</b>:4, 32; <b>43</b>:3,

</span></p>

<p class=MsoListParagraphCxSpLast style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;
margin-bottom:0in;margin-left:58.5pt;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(b)<span
style='font:7.0pt "Times New Roman"'>&nbsp; </span></span><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Contemporary
English usually leaves out the conjunction that introduces indirect discourse
noun clause: <i>“</i></span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>You know <s>that</s> your father and his
men are strong”</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> (2 Samuel <b>17</b>:8; recast as </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“Your
father and his men are strong men.”</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>).</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Specificity
for Vividness</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:.25in'><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>An appositive—or a
part of a whole joined to the whole—makes a heavier statement that creates vividness,
focuses attention, and emphasizes: <i>“</i></span><i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Keep me from <span
style='color:red'>the jaws of </span>the trap they’ve set for me”</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
(Psalm <b>141</b>:9). Those structures <i>CV </i>sometimes reformats into shorter
English ones:</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            <i>“When
<span style='color:red'>their feet </span>waded into <span style='color:red'>the
edge of </span>the water”</i> becomes <i>“when they waded into the                           water”</i>
(Joshua <b>3</b>:15; <b>4</b>:13); wading covers the feet part of the
expression and <i>“into”</i>                  covers <i>“the edge of”</i>;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            <i>“like
<s>the sin of</s> divination”</i> (1 Samuel </span><b><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>15</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:23);</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            <i>“by <s>the
edge/mouth of</s> the sword”</i> (Judges <b>4</b>:15, 16);</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            <i><s>the
people of</s> Israel</i> (Judges <b>4</b>:23; <b>10</b>:8; <b>11</b>:33);</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            <i>“gather at
<s>the door of</s> the Tent of Meeting”</i> (Numbers <b>10</b>:3, <i>etc.</i>);</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            <i>“Pardon <s>the
iniquity of</s> these people”</i> (Numbers <b>14</b>:19);</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            <i>“from <s>the
power of</s> our enemies” </i>(1 Samuel<b> 4</b>:3; cp. Psalm<b> 22</b>:20-21;
Daniel <b>6</b>:25);</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify'><b><sup><span style='font-size:14.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>         18</span></sup></b><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>I’ll
leave 7,000 in Israel who<s>se knees </s>haven’t bowed to Baal </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>and<i>
<s>whose mouths</s> haven’t </i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                  kissed
him” </span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>(1 Kings <b>19</b>:18);  </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            <i>“My eyes
have seen and heard” </i>becomes <i>“I’ve seen and heard” </i>(Job</span><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> <b>13</b>:1);</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            <i>“because
it didn’t shut <s>the doors of</s> my mother’s womb”</i> (Job </span><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>3</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:10;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“Let
the proud step on me,” </span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>instead of <i>“Let the <u>foot of the</u>
proud step on me” </i>(Psalm </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            </span></i><b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>36</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:11);
</span></p>
