margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(b) replaced by <i>festival</i> for
references to extended gatherings and occasions that involved more than a banquet,
particularly the stated feasts of Israel’s festival cycle</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.5in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>flee      </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>sometimes
replaced by<i> run away</i>, <i>escape</i>, <i>get away from</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>foe</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>       enemy</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>foot      </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>eliminated
when an example of Semitic body-part language</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>for       </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(a)<i>
</i>only as a preposition</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (b)
replaced with </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>because</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> when a
conjunction is called for. Older English used </span><i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>for</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
as the coordinate conjunction and </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>because</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
for the subordinate conjunction. Modern speech has lost the distinction, using </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>because
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>for
both functions and in the process blurring the distinction. </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:.5in'><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> Besides, the
Hebrew word (<i>for</i>) often carries an illative sense rather than a logical
sequence. The wording after <i>for</i> <u>expands</u> on the previous point
rather than gives a <u>reason</u> for it. Aside from translating classical
works, English does not use <i>for</i> as illative.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            Beyond these points lies
the English tendency to omit altogether any word for the causal connection
between two statements, coordinate or subordinate. The listener makes the
connection intuitively.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>forget</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  In
texts that speak about <u>God</u>’s <i>“forgetting sin,”</i> for example, <i>CV</i>
has used an expression like <i>“not keep in mind”</i> since God is said to be
omniscient. Note the similar handling of God’s <i>“not remembering”</i> sin, <i>etc.</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>forth</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>    (went)
out</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>forever</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>permanently</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> sometimes
used instead because English tends to take it as an absolute term whereas the
Hebrew expression means absolute within its frame of reference: Ezra <b>9</b>:12,
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>etc.</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Forever
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>and
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>forever
and ever</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'> both reduced to </span><i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>forever. </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>In
Genesis <b>17</b>:8 </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>CV</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> uses </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>ongoing</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
to describe the permanent possession of Canaan by Abraham’s descendants through
Isaac.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>foundation</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> 
<i>founding</i> as in the <i>founding of the world</i>: English uses <i>foundation</i>
for the base of a structure or what a foundation can serve as a figure for.
Foundation as <i>the act of founding</i> has fallen out of use.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>fruit</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     used
for what is soft and sweet and mostly grows on trees </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            avoided
when used more generally to mean result, as in fruit of the womb or produce
from a garden or field.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.5in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>garden</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  
One particularly noticeable feature of this version is </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Orchard
of Eden</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'> in place of the traditional </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Garden
of Eden</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'> (Genesis <b>2</b>:8-<b>3</b>:24; <b>13</b>:10? Isaiah<b>
51</b>:3? Ezekiel <b>28</b>:13; <b>31</b>:9; <b>36</b>:35). Fruit-bearing trees
are the only flora the text presents as growing there and supplying Adam and
Eve with food. The decision to shift the wording illustrates substituting words
with more specific meanings. The English word </span><i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>garden</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
can include trees if the context so indicates, but normally it refers to where
vegetables or perhaps </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>flowers </span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>grow. </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>A
similar specifying occurs with </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>said.</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.5in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>be
gathered to </span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(your ancestors)  <i>pass on</i>; a more
recognizable euphemism for dying: Genesis <b>25</b>:8, 17, 29; <b>49</b>:29,
33; Numbers <b>20</b>:24; <b>27</b>:13; <b>31</b>:1; Deuteronomy <b>49</b>:50;
Judges <b>2</b>:10; 2 Kings <b>22</b>:20. <i>CV</i> did not simply reduce the
expression to <i>die</i> because the original expression could carry something
of an implication of life after death.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.5in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>gate  
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>replaced
with <i>gateway </i>to distinguish it from a gate on hinges, especially in
Ezekiel <b>40</b>ff.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.5in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Gentiles</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> 
Only in passages cited by the New Testament does <i>Gentiles</i> for “nations,
non-Jews” show up as the <i>CV</i> translation of </span><span lang=HE dir=RTL
style='font-size:14.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>גוֹי</span><span
dir=LTR></span><span lang=HE style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'><span dir=LTR></span> </span><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>in
the Old Testament </span><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(to show OT-NT connection): Deuteronomy <b>32</b>:43;
Psalm <b>2</b>:1; <b>18</b>:49; Isaiah <b>9</b>:1; <b>11</b>:10; <b>42</b>:1, <b>49</b>:6;
Amos <b>9</b>:12. Elsewhere it is simply nations, especially since many such
usages include “nations” displaced when Israel came into the Promised Land.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>ghost</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>   replaced
with </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Spirit/spirit.</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Ghost
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>has
narrowed its meaning to something scary that comes from someone deceased.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.5in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>gird     </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(a)
allowed in 1 Kings<b> 18</b>:46; 2 Kings <b>4</b>:29;<b> 9</b>:1; Job <b>38</b>:3;
<b>40</b>:7; Isaiah <b>8</b>:9<sup>2</sup>; Jeremiah <b>1</b>:17;          Nahum<b>
2</b>:1+</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (b) put </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>x</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
on him </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (c) dress </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (d) wrap </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (e) equipped (2
Samuel <b>21</b>:16)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>girdle   waistband, broad
belt</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.5in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>give</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     (a)
frequently <i>give</i> in the normal sense</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(b) <i>allow</i>: To <i>give someone
to do something</i> means to <i>let</i> <i>them do</i> it</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.5in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(c)<i>
offer</i>, <i>make available</i> </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.5in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>glory/glorify</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>   
(a) often replaced with <i>honor</i>: as in Joshua <b>7</b>:19; Judges <b>4</b>:9;
1 Samuel <b>6</b>:5; 2 Kings <b>14</b>:10; 1 Chronicles <b>16</b>:8, 9; <b>29</b>:11;
2 Chronicles <b>5</b>:13; Job <b>29</b>:20; Psalm <b>21</b>:5; <b>29</b>:1, 2; <b>64</b>:10;
<b>66</b>:2; <b>71</b>:8; <b>76</b>:10; <b>78</b>:61; <b>79</b>:9; <b>84</b>:11;<b>
85</b>:9; <b>86</b>:9, 12; <b>96</b>:7, 8; <b>102</b>:15; <b>104</b>:31; <b>115</b>:1;
<b>145</b>:11; 1<b>48</b>:13; Proverbs <b>16</b>:31; <b>25</b>:27; Isaiah <b>14</b>:8;
<b>24</b>:15,<b> </b>16; <b>25</b>:3; <b>26</b>:8, 15; <b>42</b>:8, 12; <b>43</b>:
7, 20; <b>45</b>:25; <b>48</b>:11; <b>55</b>:5; <b>59</b>:19; <b>60</b>:7, 9,
21; <b>66</b>:5; Jeremiah <b>2</b>:11; <b>13</b>:16; <b>33</b>:9; Ezekiel <b>28</b>:22;
<b>39</b>:21; Daniel<b> 2</b>:36;<b> 7</b>:14; Haggai <b>1</b>:8</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.5in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                        </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(b)<i>
</i>often replaced with <i>beauty/beautiful</i>: Psalm <b>45</b>:13; Isaiah <b>28</b>:5;
Ezekiel <b>20</b>:6; <b>31</b>:18; Hosea <b>2</b>:3, 7; <b>10</b>:5; Micah<b> 1</b>:15;
Zechariah <b>11</b>:2</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.5in'><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                        (c)
replaced with <i>splendor</i>: Isaiah <b>60</b>:13</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      </span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>go/went</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     reserved
for leaving a point of orientation. See also </span><i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>bring/brought</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
above</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                  omitted
as an indication of ingressive action</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.75in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>go
into/went into</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>   (a) rendered </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>sleep/slept
with</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>;
the chosen English idiom appears in Genesis <b>16</b>:2, 4; <b>19</b>:7; <b>29</b>:21,
23, 30; <b>30</b>:3; <b>38</b>:9, 16; <b>38</b>:2, 9, 18, 26; Deuteronomy<b> 21</b>:13;
Judges<b> 15</b>:1; <b>16</b>:1; 2 Samuel<b> 11</b>:4; 1 Chronicles <b>7</b>:23;
Psalm <b>51</b> (heading); Ezekiel<b> 23</b>:44; Amos<b> 2</b>:7.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:67.5pt;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.5in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> (b)
in two instances rendered </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>married</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:
2 Samuel<b> 17</b>:25; 1 Chronicles<b> 2</b>:21; Note Deuteronomy <b>21</b>:13. 
</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>G</span></i><i><span
style='font-size:9.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>OD</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     
(a) capital and lower case for the true God</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (b)
lower case in reference to pagan gods </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (c)
lower case in reference to, say, </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“a powerful one”</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:
Samuel in 1 Samuel <b>28</b>:13</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (d)
lower case for leaders under God: Psalm <b>82</b>:6</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(e) When in all capitals, it covers
the Tetragrammaton: Exodus <b>23</b>:17. By custom, when reading Hebrew, the
word</span><span lang=HE dir=RTL style='font-size:14.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>אֲדֹנָי</span><span lang=HE dir=RTL
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> </span><span
dir=LTR></span><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'><span
dir=LTR></span>, <i>“my Lord,”</i> is actually said in place of the
Tetragrammaton (<i>Qere</i>) so as not to risk profaning God’s personal name.
Occasionally the Hebrew word for <i>“my Lord”</i> appears ahead of the
Tetragrammaton. To avoid saying <i>“my Lord”</i> twice in a row, the Hebrew
word for <i>“God”</i> is said instead. In the <i>ketiv, </i>the pointing for
“God” (</span><span lang=HE dir=RTL style='font-size:14.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>אֱלֹהִים</span><span dir=LTR></span><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'><span
dir=LTR></span>) is placed on the consonants of the ineffable name as a
reminder to the reader (<i>Qere</i>). We have followed English translation
tradition in presenting that combination as <i>“Lord G</i></span><i><span
style='font-size:9.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>OD</span></i><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>.”</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  
</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:.5in'><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>The combined
expression appears some 197 times, particularly in Ezekiel (125): Exodus <b>22</b>:17;
Deuteronomy <b>9</b>:26; Joshua <b>7</b>:6; Judges <b>6</b>:22; <b>16</b>:28; 2
Samuel<b> 7</b>:18, 19, 20, 22; 1 Kings <b>2</b>:26;<b> 8</b>:53; Psalm <b>71</b>:5,
16;<b> 89</b>:8; Jeremiah<b> 1</b>:6; <b>2</b>:19, 22; <b>4</b>:10; <b>7</b>:20;
<b>14</b>:13; <b>32</b>:16, 25; <b>44</b>:26; <b>46</b>:10<sup>2</sup>; Ezekiel
<b>2</b>:4; <b>3</b>:11, 27; <b>4</b>:14; <b>5</b>:7; <b>6</b>:2, 11;<b> 7</b>:2,
5; <b>8</b>:1; <b>9</b>:8; <b>11</b>:2, 7, 13, 16, 17; <b>12</b>:8, 18, 22, 25,
28; <b>13</b>:3, 8, 9, 13; <b>14</b>:4, 6, 16; <b>15</b>:6; <b>16</b>:23, 30,
63; <b>17</b>:9, 19, 22, 23; <b>18</b>:23, 30; <b>20</b>:3, 17, 30, 46, 49; <b>21</b>:24,
26, 28;<b> 22</b>:3, 19, 28; <b>23</b>:22, 32, 46, 49; <b>24</b>:2, 6, 9, 24; <b>25</b>:2,
8, 12, 15; <b>26</b>:3, 5, 15, 19; <b>27</b>:3l; <b>28</b>:2, 6, 12, 20, 24,
25; <b>29</b>:3, 8, 13, 16; <b>30</b>:1, 10, 13, 22; <b>31</b>:10, 15; <b>32</b>:3,
11, 31; <b>33</b>:11, 25; <b>34</b>:1, 11, 17, 20, 31; <b>35</b>:3, 10; <b>36</b>:2,
4, 13, 37;<b> 37</b>:3, 9, 12, 19, 20;<b> 38</b>:2, 10, 14, 17; <b>39</b>:1,
10, 13, 20, 25; <b>36</b>:2, 4, 13, 37; <b>37</b>:3, 9, 12, 20;<b> 38</b>:2,
10, 14, 17<b>; 39</b>:1, 10, 13, 20, 25;<b> 43</b>:18; <b>44</b>:6, 8, 15;<b>
45</b>:9, 15, 18; <b>46</b>:1, 16; <b>47</b>:13, 23;<b> 48</b>:29; Amos <b>3</b>:8,
11; <b>4</b>:2;<b> 6</b>:8; <b>7</b>:1, 2, 4, 5; <b>8</b>:1, 11; <b>9</b>:5, 8;
Obadiah 1; Micah <b>1</b>:2; Habakkuk<b> 3</b>:19; Zephaniah <b>1</b>:7;
Zechariah <b>9</b>:14.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>graven</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> 
replaced with <i>carved</i> as with <i>images</i>.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>great    </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>reserved
as much as possible for <u>quality</u> </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            <u>size</u>
gets covered by </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>big</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>,</span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
large</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>,</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> etc.</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>hand</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>    for
a literal <u>body part</u></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            dropped as a
Hebraism for <u>personal agency</u> (God or people)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>harlot/harlotry</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      replaced
with </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>prostitute/prostitution.
Whore</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'> carries a disgusting connotation.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>harp</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>    for
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>kenor</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
in Isaiah <b>16</b>:11; <b>23</b>:16; <b>24</b>:8; <b>30</b>:32; Ezekiel <b>26</b>:13
and when combined with <i>nibal. </i>See below under <i>lyre.</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>hate     </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>In
some texts with love-hate expressions, we have taken the usage to mean degree put
for positive-negative: cp. Genesis <b>29</b>:30 about loving Rachel and hating
Leah; Malachi <b>2</b>:1-2 about Jacob and Esau.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:.5in'><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>NOTE: This kind of
weakening, strengthening, and bending of terms comes from the Hebrew tendency
to use words for what is a lesser degree of the same thing, what grows out of
the same thing, what is like the same thing.  </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:40.5pt;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>          <i>CV</i> has
avoided using <i>hate</i> in the mouth of good people toward evil ones (except
in Psalm <b>138</b>:21, <i>“hate those who hate you”</i>), using <i>despise </i>or
perhaps <i>detest </i>in many cases. <i>CV</i> lets it stand for evil people’s
attitude toward the good, for good people’s attitude toward the beliefs and
practices of evil people.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>he</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  References
to God and angels are always masculine in scripture; hence, in this matter we
have not yielded to the modern push for using inclusive language even for God
himself. A translator is supposed to translate what the text says rather than
what he may want it to say or what the readers expect or believe.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>head(s)
of fathers’ houses(s)</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>: ancestral houses</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>heal</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     allowed
to have a wider usage than for physical wounds: heal the land</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            not
disease, which calls for </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“getting better”</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>hear</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     used
for hearing a sound, not for listening or obeying</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(whole)
heart</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'> replaced with </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“all (his/their/your/my/our)
heart”</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'> to avoid awkward alliteration</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>heaven</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  
(a) God’s dwelling place </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      (b)
the realm of the stars as in “the host of heaven”</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      (c)
where contrasts with atmosphere (Psalm <b>19</b>:1-7)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Sky</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
covers where clouds are and birds fly (perhaps where stars shine). <i>Heaven </i>has
to be retained </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      in
parallelisms with <i>sky</i>: Deuteronomy <b>33</b>:26; Psalm <b>108</b>:4; <b>148</b>:4.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>heed</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>    replaced
with </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>listen
to</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>,
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>pay
attention to</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>hence</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>   not
used</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>herald</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  messenger</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>herein</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  not
used, along with </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>therein</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>, </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>wherein</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>he’s</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     contraction
for </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>he
is,</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
<i>he has, </i>or <i>he does</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>high
places</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>  not necessarily high, as in elevated, since they were
located in towns and watchtowers (2 Kings <b>17</b>:9)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (a)
elevated places in some sense: Judges <b>5</b>:18; Habakkuk <b>3</b>:19;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (b)
rendered </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>“local shrines”</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> where the L</span><span
style='font-size:9.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>ORD</span><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
was evidently worshiped, because good Southern Kingdom kings carried on reforms
with added comments that they did not </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“take away the high
places”</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>: Asa (1 Kings <b>15</b>:14), Jehoshaphat (1 Kings<b>
22</b>:43), Jehoash (2 Kings <b>12</b>:3), Amaziah (2 Kings<b> 14</b>:4),
Azariah (2 Kings <b>15</b>:4), Jotham (2 Kings<b> 15</b>:35), the later
Manasseh (2 Chronicles <b>33</b>:17) as well as 1 Kings <b>3</b>:2, 3 (prior to
the Temple), 2 Chronicles <b>15</b>:17; <b>32</b>:12;<b> 33</b>:17; Isaiah <b>36</b>:7;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (c)
called </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>“pagan shrines”</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> where other gods were
worshiped perhaps alongside the L</span><span style='font-size:9.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>ORD</span><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:
Leviticus <b>26</b>:30; Numbers<b> 33</b>:52; 1 Kings <b>11</b>:7; <b>12</b>:31,
32; <b>13</b>:1, 32, 33; <b>14</b>:23; 2 Kings <b>16</b>:4;<b> 17</b>:9, 29; <b>18</b>:4;
<b>21</b>:3; <b>23</b>:5, 8, 9, 13, 15, 19, 20; 2 Chronicles <b>11</b>:15; <b>14</b>:3,
5; <b>17</b>:6; <b>20</b>:33; <b>28</b>:4, 25; <b>31</b>:1; <b>33</b>:3, 19; <b>34</b>:3;
Psalm <b>78</b>:58; Isaiah <b>15</b>:2; Jeremiah<b> 7</b>:31; <b>32</b>:35;
Ezekiel <b>6</b>:3, 6; <b>16</b>:16, 39; Hosea <b>10</b>:8; Amos <b>7</b>:9;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (d)
pagan shrines simply called </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“shrines”</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
because of context: 2 Kings <b>17</b>:11;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (e)
simply called </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“shrines” </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>because
unclear in 2 Kings <b>17</b>:32; 2 Chronicles <b>15</b>:17;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (f)
made into a proper name: </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Bamoth</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
Baal (Number <b>22</b>:41).</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>hin </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  
entered roughly as 1½ gallons: Exodus <b>29</b>:40<sup>2</sup>; Leviticus <b>19</b>:36;
<b>23</b>:13; Numbers <b>15</b>:4, 5, 6, 7, 9, 10; <b>28</b>:5, 7, 14*</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>holy     </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>what
has to do with God and his associates</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            what
is </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“set
apart”</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'> to a special use: Numbers <b>6</b>:20 (<i>“set aside </i>for
the priests”)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.5in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Holy
of Holies</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>   replaced with </span><i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>the Most Holy
Place</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'> for the inner sanctuary of the Tabernacle, the Temple
that replaced it, and Ezekiel’s future Temple. Newer readers might not be
acquainted with the “x of x” formula for Hebrew superlatives: Exodus <b>26</b>:33;
1 Kings <b>8</b>:6; <b>7</b>:50; <b>6</b>:16; 2 Chronicles<b> 3</b>:8, 10; <b>4</b>:10;
Ezekiel <b>41</b>:21, 23; cp. the more familiar </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“King
of Kings”</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'> and </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“Lord of Lord”</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
(1 Timothy<b> 6</b>:15; Revelation<b> 17</b>:14; <b>19</b>:16). </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>CV</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
has kept </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>the Holy Place </span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>to designate the
corresponding outer sanctuary of the Tabernacle/Temple. </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>homage</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     used
in place of </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>worship</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> when offered to other
than deity</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>homer</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  10
bushels (Leviticus <b>27</b>:16; Ezekiel <b>45</b>:11) </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:40.5pt;text-align:justify;text-indent:-40.5pt'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>horn</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>    applies
to the raised corners on an altar (Exodus <b>29</b>:12), ram’s horn used for
signaling (Exodus <b>19</b>:13), ox horns (1 Kings <b>22</b>:11), musical
instruments (replaced with </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>trumpets</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:
Leviticus <b>23</b>:24),</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>host</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>L</span></i><i><span
style='font-size:9.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>ORD</span></i><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>/God
of Hosts</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>, very frequently (with capital <u>H</u>)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>population</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:
2 Kings <b>25</b>:22</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  
         </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>great number</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>: 2 Chronicles <b>30</b>:18;
Esther <b>10</b>:3</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>army</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:
Exodus <b>6</b>:26; 1 Kings <b>20</b>:28; 2 Chronicles <b>14</b>:11; <b>32</b>:7</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            left
as </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>host
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>when
in reference to the stars as in </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“the starry host”</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.5in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>hour</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> 
used only for a specific amount of time; replaced with </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>time</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
for an indefinite amount of time (<i>vs.</i> </span><i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>time</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
for a longer time frame as </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>in the day of</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>house
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>called
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>house</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
for a private dwelling (2 Kings <b>8</b>:2; 1 Chronicles <b>13</b>:7)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            called
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>house</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
or </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>household</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
for extended families</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>called
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>house</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
with </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“ancestral
houses</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>,</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>”</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> which adds
historical lineage to present extended family (2 Kings <b>10</b>:30)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            called
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>palace</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
for royal residences (2 Kings <b>20</b>:18)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            called
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Temple</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
for Yahveh’s house (2 Kings <b>21</b>:7) or pagan temple (2 Kings <b>5</b>:18)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            called
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Tabernacle</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
sometimes for God’s house before the temple was built: 1 Samuel <b>1</b>:6, 9,                                 24;
<b>3</b>:3, 15; 2 Samuel <b>12</b>:20; 1 Chronicles<b> 6</b>:31</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            called
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>house</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
for structures/huts associated with high places (2 Kings <b>17</b>:32; <b>23</b>:19)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.5in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>household  </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>replaced
in what seem to be smaller groupings as in sacrificial offerings and Passover
observances</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>however</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>    reduced
usage; replaced by <i>but</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.5in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>humble
yourselves</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>  when combined with not working—literally <i>“afflicting
your souls”</i>—becomes <i>fasting</i> (Psalm <b>35</b>:13; Isaiah<b> 58</b>:3,
5, 10). Translations have used <i>afflict yourselves.</i></span> <span
style='font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>See Leviticus <b>16</b>:29, 30, 31;
<b>23</b>:27-28; Numbers <b>29</b>:7.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>if</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>    replaces
the more formal </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>whether </span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>for introducing
indirect questions: Ezra <b>5</b>:17 (</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“see if King Cyrus issued
a decree”</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>); Genesis <b>8</b>:8; <b>18</b>:21; <b>24</b>:49; <b>27</b>:21;
<b>37</b>:32; <b>42</b>:33; Exodus <b>4</b>:18; <b>22</b>:8; Leviticus <b>14</b>:2;
Numbers <b>11</b>:23; <b>13</b>:19<sup>3</sup>; Judges <b>2</b>:22; 1 Samuel <b>20</b>:10;
2 Samuel <b>12</b>:22; 2 Kings <b>1</b>:2; Esther <b>4</b>:14; Job <b>6</b>:28;
Psalm <b>14</b>:2; <b>53</b>:2; <b>139</b>:24; Ecclesiastes<b> 2</b>:19; <b>9</b>:1;
<b>11</b>:6<sup>2</sup>; Song of Solomon <b>6</b>:11; <b>7</b>:12; Lamentations
<b>1</b>:12; Joel <b>2</b>:14; Malachi<b> 3</b>:10. English speakers seldom
distinguish between </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>if</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> for condition and </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>whether</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
for indirect question. They use <i>if</i> to introduce both—as Greek does with </span><span
lang=EL style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>εἰ</span><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>in
order that</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>    reduced to simple infinitive to indicate purpose
whenever that is sufficiently clear</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>inquire</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  
ask the L</span><span style='font-size:9.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>ORD</span><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> <i>vs.</i>
inquire from the L</span><span style='font-size:9.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>ORD</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>iniquity</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  often
replaced by <i>sin </i></span><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>or <i>sinfulness</i> except where <i>sin </i>occurs
in close proximity</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>isn’t</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     reduced
usage, but kept particularly in third-person-singular questions.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Israelite 
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>to
reduce the frequent use of the term among a whole multitude of gentilics, </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>CV</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
has </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(a)
dropped it altogether (1 Kings <b>12</b>:24), </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(b)
replaced it with </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>people </span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(1 Kings <b>6</b>:13;
Nehemiah <b>11</b>:3; 2 Chronicles (<b>31</b>:5), </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      (c)
used </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Israel
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>instead
in very many verses like Leviticus <b>7</b>:36; <b>9</b>:3; <b>10</b>:11; <b>11</b>:1;
<b>16</b>:16, 19, 21, 34; <b>17</b>:14; <b>23</b>:43; Numbers<b> 8</b>:14; <b>9</b>:4;
<b>10</b>:12; <b>14</b>:10;<b> 19</b>:2; <b>20</b>:19; <b>21</b>:10;
Deuteronomy<b> 10</b>:6; Joshua<b> 4</b>:18;<b> 5</b>:1; <b>11</b>:11; <b>12</b>:1;
<b>14</b>:1;<b> 24</b>:32; Judges 11:16; 1 Samuel <b>29</b>:1; 2 Samuel <b>25</b>:11;
1 Kings <b>20</b>:27; 2 Kings<b> 13</b>:21; <b>18</b>:4; 2 Chronicles<b> 5</b>:10;
<b>7</b>:2; <b>8</b>:8; <b>11</b>:13; <b>13</b>:16; <b>33</b>:9; <b>34</b>:33; <b>35</b>:18;
Nehemiah <b>8</b>:14, 17; <b>9</b>:1; <b>13</b>:6; Isaiah <b>17</b>:9; Jeremiah
<b>16</b>:14; <b>23</b>:7; Ezekiel <b>2</b>:3; Hosea <b>1</b>:16, </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      (d)
used </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Israeli
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>in
Exodus <b>12</b>:42; <b>13</b>:19; <b>14</b>:2; <b>31</b>:15; Numbers <b>35</b>:29;
<b>39</b>:32; Leviticus <b>22</b>:18; <b>24</b>:10<sup>2</sup>; <b>25</b>:46,
55; Numbers <b>25</b>:6; <b>26</b>:4; <b>35</b>:15; 1 Samuel <b>13</b>:21; 2
Samuel <b>17</b>:25; <b>21</b>:2; 2 Chronicles <b>35</b>:17 Ezra<b> 3</b>:1; <b>6</b>:21;
<b>7</b>:13; Nehemiah <b>2:</b>10; <b>7</b>:79; Ezekiel<b> 4</b>:13; <b>14</b>:4;
<b>47</b>:22; Daniel <b>1</b>:3. </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>it</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>    (a)
not </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>he</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
or </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>she</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> unless
in reference to something male or female. So </span><i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“wisdom</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>,</span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>”</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
for example, would not be </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“she.”</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> English
has lost grammatical gender, and retains sex-reference only in personal,
intensive, and reflexive pronouns for the third person singular. The rest are </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“gender
neutral.”</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(b)
not used in expletive constructions involving <i>it</i>. Such formats use <i>it
</i>to indicate that the infinitive subject is coming up later: <i>“It is
necessary to obey.”</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Jehovah</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     not
used; an artificial name created from combining the consonants of the Tetragrammaton
(</span><span lang=HE dir=RTL style='font-size:14.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>יהוה</span><span dir=LTR></span><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'><span
dir=LTR></span>), God’s personal name, and the vowels from lord (</span><span
lang=HE dir=RTL style='font-size:14.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>אֲדֹנָי</span><span
dir=LTR></span><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'><span
dir=LTR></span>). The result is </span><span lang=HE dir=RTL style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>י</span><span
lang=HE dir=RTL style='font-size:14.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>ְהֹוָה</span><span
dir=LTR></span><span lang=HE style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'><span dir=LTR></span> </span><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(</span><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Jehovah).
</span><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>See
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Yahveh
infra.</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>just</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      <i>CV
</i>has allowed several misplaced <i>just</i></span><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>’s as per common
usage: Leviticus <b>11</b>:6; Numbers 23:13; Deuteronomy <b>2</b>:35; Joshua <b>17</b>:17;
2 Kings <b>5</b>:13, <i>etc.</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>kid</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> 
(a) usually in reference to a young goat: Genesis <b>38</b>:17, 20; Exodus <b>23</b>:19;
<b>34</b>:26; Deuteronomy <b>14</b>:21; Judges <b>6</b>:19; <b>13</b>:19</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      (b)
in reference in a young child: Proverbs <b>29</b>:15</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>kill</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> (a)
replaced by </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>put to death</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
in cases of official execution </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      (b)
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>murder</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
for killing people illegally</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      (c)
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>kill</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
and </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>slay</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
for animals and people loosely speaking</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>know/knew</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      not
used for sexual relations</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>lament 
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>besides
<i>lament</i>, <i>CV </i>uses <i>chant a dirge</i>, and <i>mourn.</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>land  
 </span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>sometimes
replaced with<i> country </i>(Jeremiah <b>33</b>:11)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Law</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     capitalized
in reference to the Law of Moses, particularly as the full set of requirements</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>leaven</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>   
replaced with </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>yeast</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>, </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>without
yeast</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'> (for </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>unleavened</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>              
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>leaven</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
retained only in the name for The Feast of Unleavened Bread</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>lest</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  
(a) replaced by </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>for fear that, so that . . . not </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>as
introductions to fear clauses. Ex. “lest you trip over a rock” becomes “so
you’ll never trip on a rock” (Psalm <b>91</b>:12).</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>let</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  (a)
reserved mostly for meaning “allow”</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      (b)
avoided as a translation for the Hebrew jussive, a third-person imperative:
“Let him do so and so”: Psalm <b>68</b>:1-2; <b>71</b>:13; <b>90</b>:17; <b>109</b>:7;
<b>149</b>:2-9, </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>etc.</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(c)<i>
let’s </i>for exhortation (“let us”).</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>lethech</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> entered
approximately as 5 bushels: Hosea<b> 3</b>:2*</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>lie/lay</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  the
distinction has been maintained: <i>lie</i> for intransitive, <i>lay</i> for
transitive.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>like</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      (a)
used as a preposition </span></p>

<p class=MsoListParagraph style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(b)<span
style='font:7.0pt "Times New Roman"'>&nbsp; </span></span><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>as
a conjunction in place of </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>as</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>: </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“like
God instructed him” </span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(Genesis <b>7</b>:9; 1 Samuel <b>12</b>:15;
Ezra <b>4</b>:3; Esther<b> 5</b>:8; Job <b>16</b>:4; Psalm <b>119</b>:116) </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>listen</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> 
several times omitted as a seemingly unnecessary call to attention, including Genesis
<b>23</b>:6; <b>26</b>:43; Exodus <b>4</b>:1; Deuteronomy <b>1</b>:45; <b>4</b>:30;
<b>8</b>:20;<b> 9</b>:1; <b>13</b>:2; <b>17</b>:2; 1 Kings<b> 12</b>:15; 2
Kings <b>14</b>: 11; <b>18</b>:28; 2 Chronicles<b> 13</b>:4; Psalm <b>102</b>:2;
</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>log  
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>entered
approximately as 1 pint: Leviticus <b>14</b>:10, 12, 15, 21, 24*</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Lord</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>    </span><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>capitalized
in</span><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
reference to God</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:22.5pt;text-align:justify;text-indent:-22.5pt'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>lord</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>   
(a) a term of deference to another person; an honorific; </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:22.5pt;text-align:justify;text-indent:-22.5pt'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(b)
