replaced in a few cases with </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Sir</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
(Genesis <b>19</b>:18; <b>23</b>:6, 11; <b>24</b>:11; Judges <b>4</b>:18; 1
Samuel <b>1</b>:26)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:22.5pt;text-align:justify;text-indent:-22.5pt'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      (c)
used in place of </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Lord </span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>for Zechariah’s
address to the angel in<b> 1</b>:3; <b>4</b>:1, 13; <b>6</b>:3</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>L</span></i><i><span
style='font-size:9.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>ORD</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     
replacement for God’s personal name, which became ineffable in Jewish custom
sometime before the translation of the Septuagint (LXX) a couple centuries
before Christ. </span><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>We have followed convention in using all
capitals to indicate the Tetragrammaton (</span><span lang=HE dir=RTL
style='font-size:14.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>יהוה</span><span
dir=LTR></span><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'><span
dir=LTR></span>) in the Hebrew text. See also <i>G</i></span><i><span
style='font-size:9.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>OD</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
for a listing of the texts that combine <i>Lord</i> and <i>L</i></span><i><span
style='font-size:9.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>ORD</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>.
To see most places where <i>Yahveh</i> actually occurs in the original, see
“The Yahveh Covenant,” a variant of <i>The Conversational Old Testament</i>
that retains the use of God’s personal name. </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>loud voice   </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>replaced
with <i>shout</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-indent:-.5in'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>love</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     when
possible, replaced with <i>care about</i>,<i> care </i>in order to help avoid
the modern romantic implication the English term has picked up</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>lyre</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      (<i>nibal</i>)
paired with <i>harp</i> (<i>kenor</i>) in 1 Kings <b>10</b>:12; 1 Chronicles <b>10</b>:12;
<b>13</b>:8; <b>16</b>:5; <b>15</b>:16, 21, 28; <b>16</b>:5; <b>25</b>:1, 6; 2
Chronicles <b>5</b>:12; <b>9</b>:11; <b>13</b>:8; <b>15</b>:16, 21, 28; <b>16</b>:5;
<b>20</b>:28; <b>29</b>:25; Nehemiah <b>12</b>:27; Psalm <b>33</b>:2; <b>57</b>:8;
<b>71</b>:22; <b>81</b>:2; <b>93</b>:2; <b>108</b>:2; <b>150</b>:3; Isaiah <b>5</b>:12;
Daniel <b>3</b>:5; used instead of the more familiar and general word <i>harp</i>
in Isaiah <b>14</b>:11; Amos <b>5</b>:23; <b>6</b>:5. See <i>harp</i> above for
instances rendered with harp</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>lust/s</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>    replaced
by </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>drives
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>or</span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
desires</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.5in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>man     </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(a)
retained for man or husband (</span><span lang=HE dir=RTL style='font-size:
14.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>איש</span><span
dir=LTR></span><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'><span
dir=LTR></span>) and when in contrast to woman or child;</span></p>

<p class=MsoListBulletCxSpFirst style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;
margin-bottom:0in;margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:0in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(b)
when in contrast to animal or idea, frequently replaced by <i>a person, people</i>,
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>mortal,
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>a
plural of some kind, indefinite pronouns, <i>you/they</i> advisedly, or </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>I/we</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
when the reference is not in contrast to God, woman, or child. <i>“If <s>a man</s>/someone
that’s unclean from a corpse touches any of these, will they become unclean?” </i>(Haggai
<b>2</b>:12, 13; cp. Numbers <b>35</b>:52; Isaiah <b>13:</b>14; Jeremiah <b>50</b>:16);</span></p>

<p class=MsoListBulletCxSpMiddle style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;
margin-bottom:0in;margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:0in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(c)
replaced by </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“people”</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>: Genesis <b>1</b>:26;
<b>5</b>:1, 2;<b> 6</b>:4, 13;</span></p>

<p class=MsoListBulletCxSpLast style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;
margin-bottom:0in;margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:0in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(d)
replaced by some more specific term: </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>archer </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(1
Kings 22:34), </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>troops</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>many</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>   sometimes
replaced with </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>a lot of </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>or
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>lots
of</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>marry</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>   replaces
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>take
a wife</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'> unless someone besides the future husband is making
the arrangement</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:40.5pt;text-indent:-40.5pt'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>master</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> 
(a) sometimes dropped in appositives to moderate the “heavy” feel to the
account (1 Chronicles <b>12</b>:19); see also Genesis <b>24</b>:22, 42; 2 Kings
<b>5</b>:25;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:40.5pt;text-indent:-40.5pt'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(b)</span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>replaced
with </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>husband</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:
Genesis</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'> </span></i><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>39</span></b><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:16;
Judges <b>19</b>:3, 26, 27;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:40.5pt;text-indent:-40.5pt'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (c)  replaced
with </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>owner</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
in Judges <b>19</b>:22;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:40.5pt;text-indent:-40.5pt'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (d) the same
Hebrew word (</span><span lang=HE dir=RTL style='font-size:14.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>אדון</span><span dir=LTR></span><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'><span
dir=LTR></span>) is often rendered </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>lord</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> and
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>husband.</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:40.5pt;text-align:justify;text-indent:-40.5pt'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                  </span></i><span
dir=RTL></span><i><span dir=RTL style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'><span dir=RTL></span> </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>In
</span><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Genesis
<b>18</b>:12, </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>CV</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> has Sarah say, </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“My
husband </span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>[lord] </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>is old too.”</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Sir
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>would
have been appropriate except the sentence does not carry a vocative. </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Lord</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> appears
in brackets so as not to lose the connection with 2 Peter<b> 3</b>:6, where </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Sir</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
can appear but needs </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>lord</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> bracketed in to
connect it with Genesis<b> 18</b>:12.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>may     </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(a)
</span><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>reserved
for contingent action, an oath, or a wish;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (b) replaced with
<i>can</i> for requesting permission as per informal English usage</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>maybe</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  
used in preference to <i>perhaps</i> </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.5in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>memorial
portion</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>  traditional translation replaced with </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>God’s
portion</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'> since the rest of sacrifice or sacred loaves were for
the priests to eat. The memorial terminology derives from the Hebrew root meaning
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“to
remember”</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'> (God) that labels the items involved. </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.5in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>messenger</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  
replaced with </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>ambassador </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>in
cases of official business between political entities</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.5in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>mighty</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> not
so frequently used today; </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>CV</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> often replaces with
more natural terms like <i>powerful, great, elite, </i></span><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>strong;
kept in <i>Almighty God</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-indent:-.5in'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>mine</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>   used
only as a predicate possessive adjective (</span><i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“is mine”</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>),
not as an attributive possessive adjective (</span><i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“mine eyes”</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.5pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.5pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:.5in;text-indent:-.5in'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>naïve   </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>a
term kept in Proverbs <b>7</b>:7; <b>9</b>:4, 13, 16; <b>14</b>:18 but replaced
by </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>simple</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
in Psalm <b>116</b>:6; Proverbs <b>1</b>:6; <b>8</b>:5; </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>simpleton</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
in Proverbs<b> 1</b>:22; and </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>simpleminded</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
in Job <b>5</b>:2; Proverbs <b>1</b>:32; <b>14</b>:15; <b>27</b>:12.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.5in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>name  
Name</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
appears in a host of expressions that call attention to identity: </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“My
name is feared among the nations” </span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(Malachi <b>1</b>:14). It
is not used as a manner of speaking in English. </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>CV</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
frequently replaces </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>name</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> with a pronoun: </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“<u>I</u>’m
a great King to be respected the world over!” </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Cp.
phrases like <i>“praise <u>my name</u>,”</i> which can become <i>“praise <u>me</u>”</i>;<i>
“stood in awe of me” </i>becomes <i>“showed me reverence” </i>(Malachi<b> 5</b>:9),<i>
etc</i>.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>nation</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  
often replaces </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>land</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-indent:-.5in'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>nations  </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>replaced
a few times with </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Gentiles</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>: Deuteronomy <b>32</b>:43;
2 Samuel <b>22</b>:50; Nehemiah<b> 5</b>:8; Psalm <b>2</b>:1; <b>18</b>:49;
Isaiah<b> 9</b>:1; <b>11</b>:10; <b>42</b>:1, 6; <b>49</b>:6</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> (fall
on someone’s) neck  </span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>replaced by </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>hug</span></i><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>ged</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:40.5pt;text-align:justify;text-indent:-40.5pt'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>neighbor 
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>kept
as a term for someone living nearby; otherwise replaced with </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>another,
citizen, fellow, friend, one another, someone, </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>or
omitted: Proverbs <b>26</b>:19; <b>29</b>:15; Jeremiah <b>7</b>:5;<b> 9</b>:8; <b>49</b>:10,
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>etc.</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>nor       </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>dropped
even when combined with </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>neither</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>;
replaced by </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>or</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>not       </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(a)
negatives in general appear before what they negate</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (b)
double negatives are not used, including “can’t help but.”</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (c)
not used as part of a litotes construction</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (d)
used ahead of the verb instead of the direct object</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>O</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>   (a)
usually omitted before vocatives as in current English usage: Hosea <b>5</b>:1</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      (b)
retained with <i>King</i> in Numbers <b>21</b>:17; Nehemiah <b>2</b>:3; Daniel <b>4</b>:22,
27; <b>5</b>:10, 18; <b>6</b>:15; Joel<b>                        2</b>:21 </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:22.5pt;text-align:justify;text-indent:-27.0pt'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>oak   
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>translation
used for </span><span dir=RTL></span><i><span dir=RTL style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'><span dir=RTL></span> </span></i><span
lang=HE dir=RTL style='font-size:14.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>אלּּון</span><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>in
Genesis <b>35</b>:8 (Oak of Weeping); Joshua <b>19</b>:33 (the Oak in
Zaanannim); Isaiah <b>2</b>:13; <b>6</b>:13b; <b>44</b>:14; Ezekiel <b>27</b>:6;
Hosea<b> 4</b>:13; Amos<b> 2</b>:9; Zechariah <b>11</b>:2.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      translation used for</span><span
dir=RTL></span><i><span dir=RTL style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'><span dir=RTL></span> </span></i><span
lang=HE dir=RTL style='font-size:14.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>אֵלוֹן</span><i><span
lang=HE dir=RTL style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>in
proper names in Genesis <b>12</b>:6; <b>13</b>:18; <b>14</b>:13; <b>18</b>:1;
Deuteronomy <b>11</b>:30; Judges <b>4</b>:11; 1 Samuel<b> 10</b>:3; also Oak of
the Pillar (Judges<b> 9</b>:6, 37)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      translation replaced with </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>terebinth
tree</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
for</span><span dir=RTL></span><span dir=RTL style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'><span dir=RTL></span> </span><span
lang=HE dir=RTL style='font-size:14.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>אֵלָה</span><i><span
lang=HE dir=RTL style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>in
Genesis 35:4; Judges <b>6</b>:11, 19; 2 Samuel <b>18</b>:9<sup>2</sup>, 10, 14;
1 Kings <b>13</b>:14; 1 Chronicles <b>10</b>:12; Isaiah <b>1</b>:30; <b>6</b>:13a;
Ezekiel <b>6</b>:13a; Hosea <b>4</b>:13; also the name Valley of Elah 1 Samuel <b>17</b>:2,
19; <b>21</b>:9.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      translation replaced with </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>terebinth
tree</span></i><span dir=RTL></span><i><span dir=RTL style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'><span dir=RTL></span> </span></i><span
lang=HE dir=RTL style='font-size:14.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>אֵילִים</span><i><span
lang=HE dir=RTL style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>in
Isaiah<b> 1</b>:29; <b>57</b>:5; <b>61</b>:3; Ezekiel <b>31</b>:14.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      translation replaced with </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>terebint</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>h
for</span><span dir=RTL></span><i><span dir=RTL style='font-size:14.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'><span dir=RTL></span> </span></i><span
lang=HE dir=RTL style='font-size:14.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>אַ</span><i><span
lang=HE dir=RTL style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>לָ</span></i><span
lang=HE dir=RTL style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>ה</span><i><span
lang=HE dir=RTL style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>in
Joshua <b>24</b>:26 and in the place name terebinth in Zaanannim in Joshua <b>19</b>:33.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Note: the two words here translated </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>terebinth</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> do
not refer to the same tree as the ones translated </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>oak</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
because the terebinth word occurs with the oak word in Isaiah <b>6</b>:13 and
Hosea <b>4</b>:13.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>ocean</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>   used
several times instead of <i>sea</i> when the reference seems to be broader than
the inland bodies </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      of
water known to people in Old Testament times</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Oh</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> (a)
sometimes, so spelled, for wishes: Psalm <b>25</b>:21</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      (b)
replaced by circumlocution </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      (c)
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>O
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>is
used sparingly with vocatives: Numbers <b>21</b>:17; Daniel <b>4</b>:22, 27; <b>16</b>:15;
Joel <b>2</b>:21.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>of</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>   (a)
omitted for introducing appositives when clear without </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>of</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.5in'><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      (b) replaced
as often as possible with another appropriate English preposition. The Hebrew
construct appears so often, the English </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>of</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>,
its closest equivalent, shows up very frequently, especially when combined with
other Hebrew sources for </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>of</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> in English.
Apostrophe </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>s </span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>can replace “</span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>of
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>___”
if there is no subsequent modifier on the object of the preposition: “the
family of Obed-edom” can become </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“Obed-edom’s family”</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
(1 Chronicles <b>13</b>:14); otherwise, it cannot be transformed: </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“the
house of Obed-edom the Gittite”</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> (1 Chronicles <b>13</b>:13).</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      (c)
replaced frequently with <i>’s</i> or <i>s’</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            Its
prevalence comes, again, from the extensive use of Hebrew nouns in construct.
The idiom joins two nouns for referents that have some kind of relationship:
apposition, one causing the second, one being the material the other is made of
(“an idol of wood”), one as the source of the other (Saul of Gibeah”), one
being a characteristic or status of the other (</span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“Moses,
the man of God”</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>oil</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>   
in most cases, </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>oil</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> is </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>olive
oil</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
or at least includes it. In cases where it is involved in <u>food</u>, <u>fuel</u>,
or <u>sacrifices</u>, CV has said </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>olive oil.</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
In cases of <u>anointing</u>, another ingredient was present with it, as when
called </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>anointing oil </span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(Exodus <b>25</b>:6; <b>30</b>:23-33;
<b>35:</b>8). At any rate, </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>oil</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> did not cover as
many things as modern readers might assume.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.5pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>omer/‘omer</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> 
entered approximately as ⅒ ephah/bushel: Exodus <b>16</b>:16, 18, 22, 32, 33,
36*</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.5pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>one</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      (a)
replaced by <i>someone</i>,<i> a person</i>, or some other indefinite term in
place of<i> one </i>by itself</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.5pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (b) the only
pronoun capitalized for reference to deity (One): Genesis <b>30</b>:2, </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>etc.</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>only</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     <i>CV
</i>has allowed a few “misplaced </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>only’s</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>,”
as often occurs in everyday English. The <i>only</i> occurs ahead of the verb
instead of the restricted adjective: Genesis <b>14</b>:24; Exodus <b>12</b>:20;
Numbers <b>12</b>:2; <b>22</b>:35; Joshua <b>17</b>:3; Isaiah <b>5</b>:10;
Ezekiel <b>14</b>:14, 16; Haggai <b>2</b>:16<sup>2</sup>; Zechariah <b>2</b>:8.
(cp. “misplaced </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>even</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> or </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>just</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>”
above)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>or</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  appositional
<i>or</i> not used except in 1 Chronicles<b> 11</b>:4; Ezekiel <b>45</b>:14</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>own</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     The
intensive reflexive pronouns <i>my own</i>, <i>our own</i>,<i> </i></span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>etc.</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>,
have sometimes been added in place of the Hebrew simple pronoun: <i>“My own son
wants to kill me” </i></span><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(2 Samuel <b>16</b>:11). In other places, <i>own</i>
has not been inserted with the intensive pronoun (<i>his own</i>, <i>their own</i>,
<i>etc.</i>) because it sets up too much of a contrast (<i>vs.</i> someone
else) in addition to simply “reflecting” the subject of the clause.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>parents</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  in
place of “father and mother”</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Passover  
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>CV
has adopted the expression <i>celebrate/keep Passover </i>without the article
in Numbers<b> 9</b>:2-3, 14; Deuteronomy <b>16</b>:1; Joshua<b> 5</b>:10; 2
Kings <b>23</b>:21; 2 Chronicles <b>30</b>:1, 2, 5; <b>35</b>:1, 16, 17, 18;
Ezra <b>6</b>:19</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>people
of the land</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>   common people: 2 Chronicles <b>33</b>:13,
20; 2 Kings <b>21</b>:24; <b>25</b>:3, 21; Jeremiah <b>34</b>:20</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.5in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>people</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> for
indefinite reference in the plural: indefinite reference can be the point of <i>men</i>,
<i>somebody</i>, plural articular substantive adjectives, plural articular
substantive particles; cp. <i>person</i> below</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.5pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>person/s</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>    a
replacement for <i>man/men</i> when more general than males</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.5pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>please</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  (a)
in place of <i>I beg of you</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.5pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(b)
added to soften the imperative </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.5pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>pregnant</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>    for
<i>with child</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>prince 
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>where
the word often appears, <i>CV</i> replaces it with <i>rulers </i>(Genesis <b>34</b>:1;
1 Samuel <b>10</b>:10)<i>, </i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      leaders
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(1
Chronicles <b>28</b>:1), <i>commander </i>(1 Samuel <b>18</b>:30), <i>chief </i>(Deuteronomy<b>
33</b>:16), <i>officers         </i>(1 Kings <b>9</b>:22), and <i>officials </i>(Esther
<b>1</b>:16) as well as retaining it in places like 2 Samuel <b>3</b>:38,   <i>etc.</i>
Biblical translations use <i>prince</i> for a variety of posts besides someone
in line for royalty;       retained in <i>CV</i> especially in Ezekiel and
Daniel.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>prison</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  
for lengthy incarceration; <i>jail </i>for a temporary stay</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:22.5pt;text-align:justify;text-indent:-22.5pt'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>prostitution
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> often
preceded with the added word </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>religious </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>where
prostitution serves as a picture of polytheism and religious unfaithfulness of
Yahveh. That unfaithfulness did often involve fornication. Cp, a similar issue
with </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>ceremonially
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>clean/unclean
above.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>pursue</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> 
 replaced by </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>chase</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>put
to death</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>     replacement expression for <i>slay</i> when the
execution comes at the behest of official action</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:27.0pt;text-align:justify;text-indent:-27.0pt'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>quickly</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>   
avoided whenever possible; replaced with <i>quick</i> at the front of the
sentence or <i>hurry and, soon,</i> </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      <i>right
away,</i> or<i> now</i> in the adverb position; left more often in poetic
material</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>reap</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     harvest
(Genesis<b> 26</b>:12)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>reapers  
harvest crew (Ruth <b>2</b>:3-14; 2 Kings <b>4</b>:18)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>reed/rod</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  entered
as 9ft measurements (</span><span lang=HE dir=RTL style='font-size:14.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>קָנֶה</span><span
dir=LTR></span><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'><span
dir=LTR></span>): Ezekiel <b>40</b>:3, 5, 6<sup>2</sup>, 7<sup>2</sup>, 8;<b>
41</b>:8; <b>42</b>:16<sup>2</sup>, 17, 18, 19*</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:27.0pt;text-align:justify;text-indent:-27.0pt'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>region</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  
used instead of </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>world</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> or </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>earth</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
in a few places, notably with the Babylonians and their </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      conquest
of a large swath of nations to their south (Jeremiah <b>25</b>:26; <b>51</b>:7,
49; Lamentations<b> 4</b>:12; Habakkuk <b>1</b>:6) as well as the territory
affected by the great famine in Joseph’s time (Genesis <b>41</b>:56, 57).</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>rejoice</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> 
not often used in everyday conversation; replaced with “celebrate,” “take
comfort” (Job <b>6</b>:10), “be happy” (Psalm <b>13</b>:5), “be glad” (Psalm <b>68</b>:3)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(not)
remember</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'> In places where God is said not to remember sins, for
instance, </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>CV</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> has used wording </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>like
“not keep in mind”</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> because he is said to be omniscient (Job <b>11</b>:6;
Ezekiel<b> 3</b>:20; <b>33</b>:13).</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>repent</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  
sometimes replaced in reference to God because it implies having made a
mistake: Joel<b> 2</b>:13; (Genesis <b>6</b>:6). It is either an anthropopathic
expression or the verb includes the meaning </span><i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“saddened by.”</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>revolt</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  replaces
<i>rebel</i> in international relations: Genesis <b>14</b>:4; 2 Kings <b>1</b>:1;
<b>3</b>:5, 7;<b> 8</b>:20, 22;<b> 18</b>:7, 20; <b>24</b>:1, 20; 2 Chronicles <b>21</b>:8,
10<sup>2</sup>; <b>36</b>:13; Ezra <b>4</b>:15, 19; Nehemiah <b>2</b>:19; <b>6</b>:6;
Jeremiah <b>52</b>:3*</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>righteous/righteousness    </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>replaced
as often as possible with </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>good/upright</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
and </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>goodness/uprightness.</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
The older term has become a “religious” word, sometimes used with a negative connotation.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Sabbath</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     (a)
capitalized for the Jewish Sabbath day</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      (b)
lower case for sabbath years or other usages: Leviticus <b>23</b>:24; <b>25</b>:6;
2 Chronicles <b>35</b>:21</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>sackcloth</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>   replaced
with </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>burlap</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>saints</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  
replaced with </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(his) holy people</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
because the term has been specialized for those within God’s people who meet
specific qualifications </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>salute</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  replaced
by <i>greet</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>say/said</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     (a)
left out along with other introductions to direct discourse in dialogues.
Change of lines with new quotation marks indicates the change of
speakers—unless such an arrangement creates ambiguity some way (Judges <b>19</b>:18).
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Said
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>falls
out as well ahead of direct discourse; the quoted words in quotations marks and
italics make it plain that the speaker has “said” (Judges <b>19</b>:10; Daniel <b>2</b>:24,
25).</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(b)
replaced with <i>told</i> if there is a commanding element in the context</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      (c)
often replaced with <i>ask</i> when <i>said </i>appears ahead of a question</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      (d)
replaced with other more specific words to create variety of expression: <i>shout</i>
(2 Samuel <b>10</b>:10), <i>cry out</i> (Judges <b>16</b>:20; 2 Samuel <b>18</b>:33),
<i>consult </i>(Esther <b>1</b>:13), <i>confront</i> (Judges <b>12</b>:1; Ezekiel
<b>16</b>:1), <i>confess</i> (1 Samuel <b>7</b>:6).</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      This
practice creates greater variety of expression and makes more specific
indications of action. See a similar rationale for <i>Orchard of Eden </i>above.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>saying
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>omitted
as a free-standing participle that serves as a quotation mark at the beginning
of direct discourse, especially when it makes for redundancy after </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“He
spoke to ___, saying . . . ”</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>sea</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>       (a)
used for the Mediterranean Sea, Red Sea, and the Salt Sea (Dead Sea); <i>lake</i>
covers the Sea of Chinnereth and Lake Huron.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:.25in'><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(b) <i>Ocean </i>covers
32 places that apply to more than the known bodies of water in Old Testament times:
Genesis <b>1</b>:2, 10, 22, 26, 28;<b> 9</b>:2; Exodus <b>20</b>:11;
Deuteronomy <b>30</b>:23; Nehemiah <b>9</b>:6; Job <b>9</b>:9; <b>11</b>:9; <b>36</b>:30;
<b>38</b>:8, 16; Psalm <b>8</b>:8; <b>29</b>:3; <b>33</b>:7;<b> 46</b>:2; <b>78</b>:15;
<b>89</b>:25; <b>96</b>:11; <b>104</b>:6, 25; <b>107</b>:23; <b>146</b>:6;
Proverbs <b>8</b>:29; <b>23</b>:34; <b>30</b>:19; Ecclesiastes<b> 1</b>:7<sup>2</sup>;
Isaiah <b>11</b>:9; Amos <b>5</b>:8.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:.25in'><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(c) <i>CV </i>designates
the <i>Eastern Sea </i>as the<i> Dead Sea</i>: Ezekiel <b>47</b>:18; Joel <b>2</b>:20;
Zechariah<b> 14</b>:8.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      (d) <i>CV </i>designates the<i>
(Great) Sea</i> as the<i> Mediterranean (Sea)</i>: Numbers <b>34</b>:5, 6, 7;
Joshua<b> 1</b>:4; <b>9</b>:1; <b>15</b>:11, 12, 47; <b>16</b>:3, 6, 8;<b> 17</b>:9,
10; <b>23</b>:4; Psalm<b> 80</b>:11; Ezekiel <b>47</b>:10, 15, 19, 20; Daniel <b>7</b>:2.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      (e) <i>CV </i>designates the <i>Salt
Sea</i> as the <i>Dead Sea</i>: Numbers <b>34</b>:3, 12; Deuteronomy <b>3</b>:17;
Joshua <b>3</b>:16; <b>12</b>:3; <b>15</b>:2, 5;<b> 18</b>:14.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      (f) <i>CV </i>designates the <i>Western
Sea</i> as the <i>Mediterranean Sea</i>: Deuteronomy Sea<b> 11</b>:24; <b>34</b>:2;
Joel <b>2</b>:20; Zechariah <b>14</b>:8.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>search</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> look
for</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>seed     </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(a)<i>
</i>used for plants seed</span></p>

<p class=MsoListParagraph style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(b)<span
style='font:7.0pt "Times New Roman"'>&nbsp; </span></span><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>not
used for human descendants; replaced with <i>descendants</i> (Genesis<b> 9</b>:9;
<b>12</b>:7; <b>13</b>:15), <i>offspring</i> (Genesis<b> 3</b>:15<b>; 4</b>:25),
and the like</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>seek</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     (a)
replaced by <i>look for</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoListBullet style='margin:0in;text-align:justify;text-indent:.5in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(b)
<i>try to</i>, if conative: 1 Kings <b>22</b>:25, <i>“You’ll find out when you
try to hide in some secret room.”</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>seize</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>    reduced
usage; replaced with grab, arrest, capture</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>selah</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>    the
marginal note of uncertain value used in the Hebrew text some 71 times in 36 Psalms—mostly
before Psalm <b>90</b>: <b>3</b>:2, 4, 8; <b>4</b>:2, 4; <b>7</b>:5; <b>9</b>:16,
20; <b>20</b>:3; <b>21</b>:2; <b>24</b>:6, 10; <b>32</b>:4, 5, 7; <b>39</b>:5, 11;<b>
44</b>:8; <b>46</b>:3, 7, 11; <b>47</b>:4; <b>48</b>:8; <b>49</b>:13, 15; <b>50</b>:6;
<b>52</b>:3, 5; <b>54</b>:3; <b>55</b>:7, 19; <b>57</b>:3, 6; <b>59</b>:5, 13; <b>60</b>:4;
<b>61</b>:4; <b>62</b>:4, 8; <b>66</b>:4, 7, 15; <b>67</b>:1, 4; <b>68</b>:7,
19, 32;<b> 75</b>:3; <b>76</b>:3, 9; <b>77</b>:3, 9, 15; <b>81</b>:7; <b>82</b>:2;
<b>83</b>:8; <b>84</b>:4, 8; <b>85</b>:2; <b>87</b>:3, 6; <b>88</b>:7, 10; <b>89</b>:4,
37, 45, 48; <b>140</b>:3, 5, 8; <b>143</b>:6. Also see Habakkuk <b>3</b>:3, 9,
13. The notation may indicate an emotional break or an instrumental sforzando
or interlude. The Septuagint understands it as a pause (?) (</span><span
lang=EL style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>διάψαλμα</span><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>).
<i>CV </i>omits the word throughout.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>-self</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
(a) sometimes replaces the Hebrew simple pronoun where it has a <u>reflexive
value</u>, that is, “reflects” the subject of the clause</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>       
(b) Though Hebrew does not have distinctive <u>intensive pronoun</u> forms, <i>myself</i>,
<i>themselves</i>, </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>etc.</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>, we have inserted
them into the text as per modern English usage. Hebrew uses a personal pronoun
with the subject to create something of the same value: </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“the
man he.”</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'> </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>       
(c) Reflexive pronouns are used only when they in fact “reflect” the subject of
the sentence. <i>CV</i> does not follow the habit of substituting <i>myself</i>
for <i>I</i> as if avoiding any hint of undue self-reference.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>seraphs</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      Anglicized
plural instead of </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>seraph<u>im</u></span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>serpent</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      usually
replaced by <i>snake</i>; if evidently poisonous, changed to <i>viper</i> or <i>adder</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:22.5pt;text-align:justify;text-indent:-22.5pt'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>servant  
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Sometimes
changed to a verb construction (1 Samuel <b>27</b>:12);</span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Often
omitted, especially with appositives (Genesis <b>50</b>:2; Exodus <b>14</b>:31;
Numbers<b> 12</b>:7, 1 Samuel <b>19</b>:4; Jeremiah <b>29</b>:29), or replaced
with a whole range of more specific words: </span><i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>advisor </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(2
Samuel <b>13</b>:31), </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>aide </span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(Genesis <b>39</b>:5),
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>ambassador
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(2
Samuel <b>10</b>:4), </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>assistant</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
(Exodus <b>33</b>:11), </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>attendant </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(2
Chronicles <b>9</b>:12), </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>commander</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> (1
Samuel <b>18</b>:30),</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> everyone </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(2
Samuel <b>3</b>:38), </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>foreman </span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(Ruth <b>2</b>:5),
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>the
guard</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'> (2 Samuel <b>11</b>:9), </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>herdsmen
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(Exodus
<b>9</b>:20), </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>laborer </span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(Job <b>1</b>:15),</span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> maid
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(Genesis
<b>16</b>:2); </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>men </span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(1 Samuel <b>29</b>:10),
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>messenger
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(2
Kings <b>19</b>:6),</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> officer </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(2
Samuel <b>8</b>:7), </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>official</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> (Genesis <b>41</b>:7),
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>people</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
(Leviticus<b> 35</b>:42), </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>reaper </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(Ruth
<b>2</b>:9)</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>, sailor </span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(2 Chronicles <b>9</b>:21),
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>slave
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(Jeremiah
<b>34</b>:16), </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>someone </span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(Daniel <b>10</b>:17);
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>steward
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(2
Samuel <b>19</b>:17; </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>subject </span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(Ezra <b>4</b>:11),
<i>warrior</i> (1 Chronicles <b>20</b>:8), <i>woodsman</i> (2 Chronicles <b>2</b>:8),
<i>worker</i> (Nehemiah <b>5</b>:10), <i>worshiper </i>(2 Kings<b> 10</b>:23).</span>
<span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Seemingly
every kind of subordinate person can be called a servant.</span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>The
shift in </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>CV </span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>creates more varied expression
and moderates the “heavy,” antiquated feel of the content. That practice
applies also to </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>command</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> and </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>master.
</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:22.5pt;text-align:justify;text-indent:-22.5pt'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>sex/go
into/lie with/come near/uncover the nakedness of/approach/touch   </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>covered
with a variety of expressions: </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>commit adultery with </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(Proverbs
<b>6</b>:29)</span><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%'>, </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>have
intercourse with </span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(2 Samuel<b> 12</b>:11),</span> <i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>have
sex with </span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>(Genesis <b>19</b>:32, 33, 34; <b>30</b>:16; <b>38</b>:9,
16, 18; Leviticus <b>18</b>:22; <b>20</b>:13; Judges <b>15</b>:1; <b>16</b>:1;
Isaiah <b>8</b>:3; Ezekiel <b>23</b>:6), </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>have sexual relations
with </span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>(Genesis<b> 6</b>:4; <b>16</b>:2, 4; <b>29</b>:21, 24,
30; <b>30</b>:3; <b>38</b>:2; 1 Samuel<b> 1</b>:19; 2 Samuel <b>12</b>:24; <b>20</b>:3;
1 Chronicles <b>2</b>:21), </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>have relations with </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(Genesis
<b>4</b>:25; Exodus <b>19</b>:15; Ezekiel <b>23</b>:44), </span><i><span
