style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>have
carnal relations with</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> (Leviticus<b> 19</b>:20), </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>be
with </span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>(Genesis <b>20</b>:4), 1 Samuel <b>21</b>:4; 2 Samuel <b>13</b>:20),
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>marry
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(Deuteronomy
<b>21</b>:13),</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> sleep with </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(Genesis
<b>19</b>:34, 35; <b>30</b>:16; <b>39</b>:7, 12; Job <b>31</b>:10; Amos <b>2</b>:7),
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>rape
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(Genesis
<b>39</b>:14; <b>34</b>:2; 2 Samuel <b>13</b>:12, 14); <i>touch </i>(Genesis <b>20</b>:6),
<i>violate her </i>(Deuteronomy <b>22</b>:29),</span><span style='font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'> </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>go into your father’s bed</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
(Genesis <b>49</b>:4).</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>shall</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>    (a)
not used; replaced with <i>will.</i> Older idiom called for <i>shall</i> in
first person and <i>will</i> in second and third person, and reverse usage for
emphasis. </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (b)
replaced with English imperatives to cover Hebrew’s idiomatic usage of the
imperfect to indicate command or prohibition. <i>“Thou shalt not kill”</i>
becomes <i>“Do not commit murder.”</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:.25in'><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>NOTE: For the last
couple generations, English speakers have not used <i>shall</i> for first
person and <i>will</i> for second and third person—with reversal for emphasis.
Now, <i>will</i> covers all three persons and there is no emphasis by reversal;
<i>shall</i> falls out altogether. Calling attention to that shift would not be
necessary except for familiar biblical texts like the Ten Commandments—<i>“Thou
shalt not steal.”</i> <i>“Don’t steal”</i> gets rid of the archaic second
person singular pronoun <i>thou</i>, replaces imperatival future with plain
imperative, and eliminates the rare use of a pronoun with an imperative
form—except occasionally for an especially strong demand.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>ship</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     used
for larger seagoing vessels; <i>boat</i> covers smaller ones. </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(the)
Shephelah</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>     The Hebrew word does not appear in most English
translations. It refers to the line of foothills between Israel’s coastal plain
and the central hill country. <i>CV</i> covers it with the expression <i>“the
western foothills”</i> (<sup>+</sup>Deuteronomy <b>1</b>:7;<sup> </sup>Joshua <b>9</b>:1;
<b>10</b>:40; <b>11</b>:2, 16<sup>a</sup>; <b>12</b>:8; <b>15</b>:33; Judges <b>1</b>:9;
1 Kings <b>10</b>:27; 1 Chronicles <b>27</b>:28; 2 Chronicles <b>1</b>:15; <b>9</b>:27;
<b>26</b>:10; <b>28</b>:18; Jeremiah <b>17</b>:26; <b>32</b>:44; <b>33</b>:13;
Obadiah 19; Zechariah <b>7</b>:7) everywhere except for Joshua <b>11</b>:16<sup>b</sup>
<i>(“lowland,”</i> because it apparently designates an eastern territory
between Mt. Seir and Mt. Hermon).</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>skillful</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> replaced
with <i>skilled </i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>sky</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>       replaces
<i>heaven </i>for most references to other than God’s <i>“dwelling place”</i>;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>kept
in the singular; the plural perhaps became customary in English because the
Hebrew word is dual</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>slay</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      replaced
by <i>put to death</i> as official action. Alternative expressions are <i>kill</i>,
<i>slaughter</i> for animals and extreme casualties of warfare, and murder for
personal vendettas. </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>sleep</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>   (a)
not retained as a euphemism for <i>death</i> and <i>die</i> even though it is
standard wording in 1 &amp; 2 Kings, 2 Chronicles: 1 Kings <b>1</b>:21; <b>2</b>:10;
<b>11</b>:43; <b>14</b>:20, 31; <b>15</b>:8, 24; <b>16</b>:6, 28; <b>22</b>:40,
50; 2 Kings <b>8</b>:24; <b>10</b>:25; <b>13</b>:9, 13; <b>14</b>:16, 22, 29; <b>15</b>:7,
22, 38; <b>16</b>:20; <b>20</b>:21; <b>21</b>:18; <b>24</b>:6; 2 Chronicles <b>9</b>:31;
<b>12</b>:16; <b>14</b>:1; <b>21</b>:1; <b>26</b>:23; <b>27</b>:9; <b>28</b>:27;
<b>32</b>:33; <b>33</b>:20 (<i>“He slept with the ancestors”</i>). In shorter
form, it happens also in Job<b> 3</b>:13?; Psalm <b>76</b>:5, 6; <b>78</b>:64; <b>90</b>:5;
Ecclesiastes <b>8</b>:16?; and Daniel <b>12</b>:2.*</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      (b) Retained as a euphemism for
sexual relations in Genesis <b>19</b>:8; <b>38</b>:26; 2 Samuel <b>11</b>:4;
Psalm<b> 51</b> heading; Amos <b>2</b>:7; Jonah <b>1</b>:5, 6*</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>smite</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>    replaced
with <i>strike</i>: Zephaniah <b>3</b>:14</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>sojourn</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      replaced
with <i>live a while</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.5in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>so</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  (a)
used for <u>purpose</u> and not preceded by a comma: <i>“Bring them to me so I
can bless them”</i> (Genesis <b>48</b>:9);</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(b) used for <u>consequence</u>
with a comma preceding: <i>“The L</i></span><i><span style='font-size:9.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>ORD</span></i><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
had given the Egyptians a favorable attitude toward them, so they let them have
what they asked for”</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> (Exodus <b>12</b>:36); a semi-colon
appears if punctuated subunits occur nearby. <i>So,</i> for an even greater
break akin to <i>therefore</i> plus comma.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(c) often left out
with consequence value as <i>because </i>has often been omitted when carrying
causal value;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(d) expressed with
<u>degree</u> and no comma: “<i>That’s why he was so angry with them”</i> (Zechariah
<b>7</b>:12);</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      (e)
not used for <u>manner</u></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>son</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      (a)
reserved, whenever possible, for direct male offspring. </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>replaced with <i>descendants</i> if
for more distant “sons.” This variance affects the interpretation and
translation of 2 Samuel <b>21</b>:15-22 as to whether David and his troops
killed four sons of Goliath or four descendants of “Rapha,” taken as a class
noun for a group of giants at Gath rather than as a descriptor of Goliath the
giant. 1 Chronicles<b> 20</b>:5 speaks of Lahmi the brother of the giant of
Gath, which means that the four in the summary of 2 Samuel <b>21</b>:22 would
not be <u>sons</u> of “Goliath” but <u>descendants</u> of “Rapha.” The parallel
text in 1 Chronicles <b>20</b>:4-8 is correspondingly affected.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            Phrases
like </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>sons
of disobedience</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> mean people characterized by disobedience.
We have sought an alternative expression like <i>disobedient people</i> or some
appropriate circumlocution.</span></p>

<p class=MsoListParagraphCxSpFirst style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;
margin-bottom:0in;margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(b)<span
style='font:7.0pt "Times New Roman"'>&nbsp; </span></span><u><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Son</span></u><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
as a reference to Messiah is capitalized: <i>“You are my Son”</i> (&lt; Psalm <b>2</b>:7).</span></p>

<p class=MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;
margin-bottom:0in;margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(c)<span
style='font:7.0pt "Times New Roman"'>&nbsp;&nbsp; </span></span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Sons
of the prophets</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> in 2 Kings is replaced by <i>the school
of the prophets</i>: <b>2</b>:3, 5, 7, 15; <b>4</b>:1, 38, (39); <b>5</b>:22; <b>6</b>:1;
<b>9</b>:1</span></p>

<p class=MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;
margin-bottom:0in;margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(d)<span
style='font:7.0pt "Times New Roman"'>&nbsp; </span></span><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>replaced
with <i>child</i>, <i>etc.</i>, in the interests of inclusive language for
singular usages. However, in Proverbs it is retained in the vocative because
the document purports to be addressed to a son by a father. Where <i>my</i>
precedes the vocative, <i>CV</i> drops it as less idiomatic for modern English.</span></p>

<p class=MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;
margin-bottom:0in;margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(e)<span
style='font:7.0pt "Times New Roman"'>&nbsp;&nbsp; </span></span><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>left
as <i>sons of</i> when it carries the loose notion of <i>those associated with
something </i>or<i> someone</i>, phrases like sons of the sanctuary, sons of
the kingdom.</span></p>

<p class=MsoListParagraphCxSpLast style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;
margin-bottom:0in;margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(f)<span
style='font:7.0pt "Times New Roman"'>&nbsp;&nbsp; </span></span><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>rendered
<i>successor</i> in Daniel <b>5</b>:22 regarding Belshazzar because there were
four kings between him and Nebuchadnezzar to whom he is connected in the
passage. Belshazzar was actually the son of Nabonidus, a revolutionary that
seized the throne in Babylon.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.5in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>sons-in-law</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     so
spelled in keeping with formal practice despite the common use of the term as a
single word in everyday speaking: Genesis <b>19</b>:14; <b>46</b>:26; Ruth <b>1</b>:6,
7, 8.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>sound</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  (a)
the translation for <i>kol</i> (</span><span lang=HE dir=RTL style='font-size:
14.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>קול</span><span
dir=LTR></span><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'><span
dir=LTR></span>) when not referring to a person’s voice</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (b)
rendered <i>voice</i> when involving a person</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (c)
omitted entirely in phrases like </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“listen to <s>the voice
of</s> the L</span></i><i><span style='font-size:9.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>ORD</span></i><i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>.”</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>spirit/Spirit 
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(a)
capitalized in reference to deity</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (b)
lower case for demeanor/attitude (Numbers <b>14</b>:24), </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (c)
an aspect of a human person, or </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:67.5pt;text-align:justify;text-indent:-31.5pt'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(d)
angels. In this last sense, </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>CV </span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>has rendered 1
Kings <b>22</b>:24 </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>spirit </span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>because of the
preceding discussions in the L</span><span style='font-size:9.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>ORD</span><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>’s throne room,
particularly <b>22</b>:21-23, though the human false prophet would not have
known about that heavenly interaction.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (e)
translated <i>wind</i>: Jonah <b>1</b>:4; 2 Kings <b>2</b>:14?</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (f)
rendered <i>breath</i>: Genesis <b>7</b>:15</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>stand
in front of</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>   replaced by </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>withstand</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
(Exodus<b> 9</b>:11; Job <b>41</b>:10)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            understood
to mean </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>serve</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> (Number<b> 5</b>:16;
Psalm <b>69</b>:19)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>stranger</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>    foreigner</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(beginning
of ­­__) strength</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> (as a male sexual reference)   expressed
as </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>the
one that first issued from                      x’s “virility”</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:
Genesis <b>49</b>:3 (Reuben to Jacob); Deuteronomy <b>21</b>:17 (any father to
his oldest                  son)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>strong
drink</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>   replaced with </span><i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>alcohol</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>supplication</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>    avoided;
replaced by </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>prayer</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>, </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>petition</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>,
or </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>plea</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>swear 
CV </span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>lets</span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>oath
formulas stand as worded instead of streamlining them all to a simpler
equivalent like </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>I swear</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>. The wide variety
of related oath forms include:</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:58.5pt;text-align:justify;text-indent:-22.5pt'><i><span

sure as the L</span></i><i><span style='font-size:9.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>ORD</span></i><i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> lives </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(1
Samuel <b>19</b>:6; <b>28</b>:20; 1 Kings <b>22</b>:14; 2 Kings <b>5</b>:16; Jeremiah
<b>4</b>:2; <b>5</b>:2; <b>16</b>:14; Hosea <b>4</b>:15), </span></p>

the Israelites out of Egypt </span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(Jeremiah <b>16</b>:14; <b>23</b>:7),<i>
</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;

arranged by a third party </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>talk</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> <i>to</i>  replaces
<i>talk with</i>: Judges<b> 14</b>:7<i>   </i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>tell
  </span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>replaced
by a more specific term like </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>explain </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(1
Samuel <b>9</b>:19), </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>inform </span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(1 Kings<b> 1</b>:27),
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>let
(you) know </span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(1 Samuel <b>19</b>:3), </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>warn
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(1
Samuel <b>3</b>:13)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>teraphs</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      Anglicized
plural in place of <i>teraph<u>im</u></i>; but replaced with <i>household gods</i>
in Kings <b>23</b>:24; Zechariah <b>10:</b>2; and <i>household idols</i> in
Genesis <b>31</b>:19, 34; Judges <b>18</b>:14, 17, 20; 1 Samuel <b>19</b>:13,
16; Ezekiel <b>21</b>:21; Hosea <b>3</b>:4</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>than</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     (a)
sometimes used as a preposition with objective case: </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Genesis
<b>39</b>:9;<i> </i>Job <b>30</b>:1 (<i>“People younger than me make fun of me”</i>);<b>
32</b>:4; Numbers <b>14</b>:21; Psalm <b>61</b>:2; Jeremiah<b> 31</b>:11). The
tendency to conceive of <i>than</i> as a preposition shows up, for instance, in
the March/April issue of the <i>Reader’s Digest</i>, p4, <i>“Our neighbor, a
much better gardener than me . . .”</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(b)
otherwise, a coordinate conjunction with same case after as before: </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“There’s
<u>nobody</u> greater in this house than <u>I</u> am”</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
(Genesis <b>39</b>:9), but not without a subsequent pro-verb (</span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“There’s
nobody greater than I”</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>; pedantic). </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>that/this</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     (a)
<i>that </i>as a demonstrative for aforesaid (<i>vs.</i> <i>this</i> as
demonstrative for to be said): Ezra <b>10</b>:5; Joshua <b>10</b>:13;
Ecclesiastes<b> 2</b>:15; Isaiah <b>58</b>:5</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (b)
(</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>that</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>)
distant demonstrative</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (c)
(</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>that</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>)
conjunction almost always omitted ahead of indirect discourse</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (d)
(</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>that</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>)
relative pronoun usually omitted in relative clauses when <i>that</i> is the direct
or indirect object within its clause</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>the</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>        (a)
usually covers the Hebrew article</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      (b) replaced
with <i>a</i> when the Hebrew article indicates what is definite to the speaker
though not the hearer. That is the rationale for saying, </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“A
virgin will conceive . . . ”</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> (Isaiah <b>7</b>:14; cp.
Genesis <b>35</b>:4; 2 Kings <b>21</b>:7). Another way to put it is to consider
the article as with a noun typical of its class—a class with only one</span><i><span
style='font-size:14.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> </span></i><span
lang=HE dir=RTL style='font-size:14.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>עלמה</span><span
dir=LTR></span><span lang=HE style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'><span dir=LTR></span> </span><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>in
it if the term </span><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>i</span><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>s understood the way the
LXX translators took it and used </span><span lang=EL style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>παρθένος</span><span
lang=EL style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
</span><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>to
cover it (as cited in Matthew <b>1</b>:23). </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      (c) replaced with <i>a</i> or a
plural noun when denoting a noun typical of its class (See <i>supra</i> under “Features
of Contemporary English.”)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:22.5pt;text-align:justify;text-indent:-22.5pt'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>thence</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  not
used, along with </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>hence</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>, </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>whence</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>,
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>hither</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>,
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>thither</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>,
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>whither</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>,
and perhaps other archaic directional terms.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>there</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>    (a)
used mostly as an adverb for location: </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“I buried Leah there” </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(Genesis
<b>49</b>:31, </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>etc.</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>).</span></p>

<p class=MsoListParagraph style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:.75in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(b)<span
style='font:7.0pt "Times New Roman"'>&nbsp; </span></span><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>reduced
usage as an expletive compared to conversational usage. In such cases, <i>there</i>
indicates that the subject is coming up later: </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“There’s
no pasture in Canaan”</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> (Genesis <b>47</b>:4; Exodus<b> 7</b>:19,
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>etc.</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>).</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>therefore</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>   replaced
with <i>so</i> or<i> </i>omitted</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>therein</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>   
not used</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>thereupon</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
not used</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>they</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     sometimes
used advisedly, that is, as equivalent to an indefinite pronoun: <i>“He won’t
have anyone to sit on David’s throne. They’ll toss his dead body out to the
heat of the day and the frost of the night</i> (Jeremiah<b> 36</b>:30).</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>this/that</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>This</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
is a near demonstrative that we have also applied to what is to be said in
contrast to <i>that</i> a distant demonstrative we have applied what was
aforesaid.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>this
day</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     today</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>this
night</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>   tonight</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>thither</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>       not
used</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>tithe     </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>replaced
with <i>tenth</i></span><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> when obviously referring to non-coinage;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            tithe</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
retained when a more general reference to the concept of </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“the
sacred portion”</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> (Numbers <b>18</b>:29): Numbers <b>18</b>:21,
26, 28, 29; Deuteronomy<b> 12</b>:6; 2 Chronicles <b>31</b>:6, 11, Nehemiah <b>10</b>:37,
38<sup>2</sup>; <b>12</b>:44; Amos<b> 4</b>:4; Malachi <b>3</b>:8, 10</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>transgress/transgression/transgressor 
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>often
replaced with shorter <i>sin/sinner</i> except where <i>sin/sinner</i> occurs
nearby</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>treacherous  
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>replaced
by </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>devious</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
because </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>treacherous </span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>has come to be associated
with impersonal things. </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Treachery</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>,
however, has been retained as a kind of thing persons do: Isaiah <b>33</b>:1, </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>etc.</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>tremble</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Fear
and trembling</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> has more the value of </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“seriously
respect</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>,</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>”</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> as shown by
Daniel <b>6</b>:26; but </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>CV has retained </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>the
familiar expression. Aside from the set expression actual “trembling” is
probably exaggerated for emphasis.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>unclean</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  
expanded to <i>ceremonially unclean</i> to clarify that the subject is not
particularly a matter of being dirty; left as <i>unclean </i>in contexts
dealing with leprosy<i> </i>and bodily fluids</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>unicorn    
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>the
King James term is replaced with <i>wild ox </i>(Deuteronomy <b>33</b>:17; Job <b>39</b>:9,
10; Psalm <b>22</b>:21;<b> 29</b>:6; <b>92</b>:10; Isaiah <b>34</b>:7)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>upon</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>    replaced
with <i>on</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>unto</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     replaced
with <i>to</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>utter    </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>as
a verb, replaced by <i>say</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>utterly</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  replaced
with <i>really</i> or another modern elative superlative equivalent</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>valor    </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>especially
in the recurrent expression </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“mighty men of valor” </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(18
times). </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>CV</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> usually replaces that phraseology
with </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“brave
soldiers,”</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'> though not exact equivalents.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>vengeance</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> 
replaced by <i>revenge</i></span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>very</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     generally
omitted along with other superlatives as part of modern English idiom that
calls for restrained expression</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      omitted <i>very</i>: Genesis <b>13</b>:2;
<b>15</b>:1; <b>27</b>:54; Exodus <b>19</b>:16; Deuteronomy<b> 7</b>:7; <b>13</b>:6
(<i>“<s>very</s> close friend”</i>); Joshua <b>23</b>:1; Judges <b>6</b>:6; <b>18</b>:9;
1 Samuel<b> 2</b>:22;<b> 5</b>:19; <b>17</b>:24; 2 Samuel<b> 11</b>:2; <b>13</b>:3;
<b>19</b>:32; <b>18</b>:17; 1 Kings <b>1</b>:4, 15; <b>10</b>:2; <b>4</b>:29; 2
Kings <b>2</b>:19; 1 Chronicles <b>18</b>:8; <b>20</b>:2; <b>22</b>:15; 2
Chronicles<b> 1</b>:1; <b>7</b>:8; <b>9</b>:1, 9; <b>25</b>:10; Job <b>8</b>:7;
Jonah <b>1</b>:4; similarly in Zechariah <b>8</b>:1, <i>‘I’m <s>extremely</s>
jealous for Zion; with great wrath I’m jealous for it.’ </i> </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>vessel</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> 
jar, basin, container, item, article, cup</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>voice    </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(a)
used only for human audible expression</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (b)
replaced with <i>sound</i> in passages that refer to other than human audible
expression</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            (c)
omitted in phrases like </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“the <s>voice of the</s> L</span></i><i><span
style='font-size:9.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>ORD</span></i><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>”</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> (as
in eliminating body-part language and moving away from part-of-whole
expressions), lifted up his voice and wept (sobbed, cried out loud, wailed;
Genesis <b>39</b>:15</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>votive</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(offering)
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> phrased
as </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>an
offering to fulfilling a vow</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>: Leviticus<b> 7</b>:16;<b>
22</b>:18; Numbers <b>29</b>:39; Deuteronomy <b>12</b>:6, 17, 26;<b> 23</b>:18</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>wait
on (him)</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>   replaced with </span><i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>depend on</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:
Jeremiah<b> 7</b>:10; Lamentation <b>3</b>:25</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>walk</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
replaced by <i>follow</i> if not physical walking: 1 Kings <b>16</b>:31; <b>21</b>:42;
2 Kings <b>8</b>:10; <b>10</b>:31; <b>17</b>:19;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>        
replaced by </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>live </span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>in Psalm <b>119</b>:45;
Proverb<b> 10</b>:9;<b> 11</b>:19; <b>13</b>:19.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>warrior</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  soldier</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:27.0pt;text-align:justify;text-indent:-27.0pt'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>water</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>   always
given as singular; the habit of referring to “waters” may come from the fact
that the Hebrew word for water is </span><span lang=HE dir=RTL
style='font-size:14.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>מים</span><span
dir=LTR></span><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'><span
dir=LTR></span>, a dual form</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>weep/wept</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>       reduced
usage; to avoid the less current expression, replaced most often by <i>cry </i></span><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(Isaiah
<b>33</b>:7, <i>etc.</i>), then by <i>sobbing </i>(Isaiah <b>65</b>:19), <i>grief
</i>(Ezra <b>3</b>:13), <i>pine away </i>(Job<b> 30</b>:31); let stand in the
alliterative <i>weep and wail</i> (Jeremiah <b>9</b>:10).</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>western
foothills </span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>our rendering of the Shephelah, a series of foothills
that run along between the Maritime Plain and the hill country to the east (2
Chronicles<b> 1</b>:15). The Shephelah is not a name that appears on typical
maps of the Holy Land (cp. Jeremiah <b>33</b>:13).</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>wherein</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     not
used</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>whereupon</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
not used</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>whether</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
replaced by <i>if </i>for introducing indirect questions</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>who</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     replaced
most of the time with <i>that</i> (except in non-restrictive clauses)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>whoever</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>    instead
of <i>whomever </i>(Exodus <b>4</b>:13, </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“Send </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>the
message </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>by whoever else you want to.”</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:22.5pt;text-align:justify;text-indent:-22.5pt'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>whom</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  dropped
completely. Since English has lost case usage except in pronouns, English
speakers have become inept at using the “right” form of them. </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Who
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>is
one of those pronouns that people constantly “misuse.” We have freely misused
it as in </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>“Who did you see?”</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> rather than </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“Whom
did you see?”</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> To avoid <i>whom</i>, we have even broken
a rule about relative clauses by using <i>that</i> to introduce non-restrictive
clauses: Daniel<b> 9</b>:21. </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“Who will I dread?”</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
(Psalm <b>27</b>:1); </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“sand by the sea, that you can’t measure” </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(Hosea
<b>1</b>:10). See comments under “English Changes” above.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>wicked/wickedness  
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>greatly
reduced in usage to match contemporary English—one that, when, used, connotes
extreme degree; </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>replaced
by several terms: </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>corrupt</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> (in connection
with rulers), </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>perverse, evil, criminal, sin, sinful, sinfulness,
sinners, evildoers</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>; sometimes dropped if not needed (1
Samuel <b>30</b>:22); kept in contexts about homosexuality (Genesis <b>13</b>:13;
<b>19</b>:7; Judges <b>19</b>:23; <b>20</b>:3, 12)).</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>wilderness  
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>often
in reference to relatively uninhabited areas perhaps because they were deserts</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      Wilderness</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
kept when part of a place designation: </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Wilderness</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
of Paran (Genesis <b>21</b>:20-21), </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>etc.</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      wilderness</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
aside from place designations: Ezekiel <b>29</b>:5; Hosea <b>2</b>:3, 14; </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>etc.</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      c</span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>ountry</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:
1 Samuel <b>4</b>:8, </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>etc.</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>desert</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:
Numbers <b>14</b>:16, </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>etc.</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>, where the people
of Israel were during their forty-year peregrination and where an actual desert
situation apparently existed</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>countryside</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>:
Judges <b>8</b>:7, 16; <b>17</b>:28; <b>20</b>:32; <b>25</b>:4; Song of Solomon<b>
8</b>:5; Jeremiah <b>48</b>:6; Joel <b>1</b>:19; </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>etc.</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>,
where pasturing places were involved.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.5in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>open
country</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>: 1 Samuel <b>13</b>:18; <b>17</b>:28, where a wider
expanse than one person’s own grazing area seems to be involved.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>wither  </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>wilt
(Psalm <b>1</b>:3)</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>woe
to</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> 
replaced with </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>misfortune awaits</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>word</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>    English
usually uses </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>word</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> to refer to an
individual grammatical word. </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>CV</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
has used a number of replacements for the same Hebrew term </span><span
lang=HE dir=RTL style='font-size:14.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>דברים</span><span
dir=LTR></span><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'><span
dir=LTR></span>, including </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>message</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>,
</span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>memoirs</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
(in the title of Nehemiah because it is written in the first person), </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>chronicles
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>(2
Kings <b>23</b>:28, </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>etc.</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>; see above under
“Italics: added words”)).</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>worship</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> (a)
reserved for deity. In today’s English, </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>worship</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
may describe exaggerated respect for someone, but that does not come up in the
biblical text.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      (b) sometimes replaced by
phraseology like <i>“bowed in homage”</i> when indicating other than deity</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>worthy</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>       replaced
with </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>deserve</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
whenever possible</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>wrath
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>  a
word less used today; replaced with </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>anger </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>where
possible</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></i></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Yahveh
</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>     replaced
with </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>L</span></i><i><span
style='font-size:9.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>ORD</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
in all caps of two sizes; </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>G</span></i><i><span
style='font-size:9.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>OD</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
in all caps of two sizes likewise replaces God’s personal name where it occurs
beside the Hebrew word for </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>lord</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>,
a combination that occurs especially in Ezekiel: <u>Exodus</u> <b>23</b>:17; <u>Deuteronomy</u>
<b>9</b>:26; <u>Joshua</u> <b>7</b>:7; <u>Judges</u> <b>6</b>:22; <b>16</b>:18;
<u>2 Samuel</u> <b>7</b>:18, 20; <u>1 Kings</u> <b>2</b>:26; <b>8</b>:53; <u>Psalm</u>
<b>7</b>:15, 16;<b> 89</b>:8; <b>141</b>:8; <u>Isaiah</u> <b>1</b>:24; <b>3</b>:1,
15; <b>10</b>:16, 23, 33; <b>19</b>:4; <b>22</b>:5, 12, 15; <b>28</b>:16, 22; <b>30</b>:15;
48:16; <b>49</b>:22; <b>50</b>:4; <b>56</b>:8; <b>61</b>:1, 11; <b>65</b>:13,
15; <u>Jeremiah</u> <b>1</b>:6; <b>2</b>:19, 22;<b> 4</b>:10; <b>14</b>:3; <b>32</b>:17,
25;<b> 44</b>:26; <b>46</b>:10<sup>2</sup>; <u>Ezekiel</u><b> 2</b>:4; <b>3</b>:11,
27;<b> 4</b>:14;<b> 5</b>:7; <b>6</b>:3, 11; <b>7</b>:2; <b>8</b>:1; <b>9</b>:8;
<b>11</b>:13, 16, 21; <b>12</b>:10, 18, 23, 28; <b>13</b>:3, 9, 13; <b>14</b>:4;
<b>15</b>:6; <b>16</b>:23, 30, 63; <b>17</b>:9, 19; <b>18</b>:23; <b>20</b>:3,
27, 30, 47, 49; <b>21</b>:24, 28, 46, 49; <b>22</b>:3, 19, 28;<b> 23</b>:22,
32; <b>24</b>:3, 6, 9, 24; <b>26</b>:3, 15, 19; <b>27</b>:3; <b>28</b>:2, 6,
12, 22, 24, 25; <b>29</b>:3, 8, 13, 16; <b>30</b>:2, 10, 13, 22; <b>31</b>:10,
15; <b>32</b>:3, 11, 31; <b>33</b>:11, 25; <b>34</b>:2, 11, 17, 20, 31; <b>35</b>:1,
11; <b>36</b>:2, 4, 13, 37; <b>37</b>:3, 9, 12, 19, 21; <b>38</b>:3, 10, 14,
17; <b>39</b>:1, 10, 13, 20, 25; <b>43</b>:18; 4<b>5</b>:15; <b>46</b>:1, 16; <b>47</b>:13,
23; <b>48</b>:29; <u>Amos</u> <b>3</b>:8, 11; <b>4</b>:2;<b> 6</b>:8; <b>7</b>:1,
2, 4, 5; <b>8</b>:1, 11; <u>Obadiah</u> 1; <u>Habakkuk</u> <b>3</b>:19; <u>Zephaniah</u><b>
1</b>:7.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                  </span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>The
exception to </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>L</span></i><i><span style='font-size:
9.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>ORD</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
in all caps is using </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;
font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Yahveh</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> in some seventy
places that stress the fact that Yahveh is his personal name: Exodus <b>6</b>:2-3;<b>
6</b>:15-16; Psalm <b>68</b>, Isaiah <b>18</b>:7<sup>2</sup>; <b>42</b>; <b>47</b>:4;
<b>51</b>:15<sup>2</sup>;<b> 54</b>:4-8; Jeremiah <b>10</b>:16; <b>15</b>:16; <b>23</b>:8;
<b>26</b>:9-24; <b>31</b>:35; <b>32</b>:18; <b>33</b>:16; <b>44</b>; <b>46</b>:18;
<b>49</b>:15; <b>50</b>:34; <b>51</b>:19, 57; Ezekiel <b>48</b>:35; Hosea <b>12-13;
</b>Amos <b>9</b>:5-6. In Psalm <b>68</b>:4 the shortened form </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>Yah</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
appears.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>CV</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
has used </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:
"Times New Roman",serif'>v</span></i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> instead of the more common </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>w</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
in transliterating the personal name of the God of Israel. That is the more
common way of pronouncing </span><i><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:
115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>vav</span></i><span style='font-size:
12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'> in modern Hebrew.</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:0in;margin-bottom:0in;
margin-left:.25in;text-align:justify;text-indent:-.25in'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>yes</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>       supplied
in place of restating the Hebrew verb, using the phrase </span><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>“behold”</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>
or replacing some longer expression: Genesis <b>18</b>:15; <b>22</b>:17, 11; <b>24</b>:58;
<b>27</b>:1, 18, 24; <b>29</b>:5; <b>46</b>:2; Exodus <b>3</b>:4; Judges <b>13</b>:11;
<b>20</b>:23, 28; 1 Samuel <b>3</b>:4, 9, 10, 16; <b>9</b>:12; <b>16</b>:5; <b>22</b>:12;
<b>23</b>:2, 12; <b>30</b>:8; 2 Samuel <b>1</b>:7; <b>2</b>:20; <b>5</b>:19; <b>12</b>:19;
1 Kings <b>22</b>:6; Jeremiah <b>1</b>:12; <b>3</b>:24; cp. Jeremiah <b>11</b>:5
(“amen”)</span></p>

<p class=MsoListBullet style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>you</span></i><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>      sometimes
used advisedly, that is, as an indefinite pronoun: “<i>even though it’s more
numerous than locusts you can’t count” </i>(Hosea <b>1</b>:10; cp. 2 Samuel <b>17</b>:19;
Job <b>41</b>:32; Jeremiah <b>46</b>:23;).</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify;text-indent:
.5in'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>A
sizable set of additional words often used in Bible translation has been
omitted, replaced with some expressions, or reduced in usage. </span><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>            </span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify;text-indent:
.5in'><span style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>                                                                                                                                
</span><span style='font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>christir.org</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-bottom:0in;text-align:justify'><span
style='font-size:12.0pt;line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></p>

<p class=MsoNormal style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-22.3pt;margin-bottom:
0in;margin-left:0in;text-align:justify'><b><span style='font-size:12.0pt;
line-height:115%;font-family:"Times New Roman",serif'>&nbsp;</span></b></p>
</div>
